        workstream_id: Option<&str>,
    ) -> Result<AgentResponse>
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment:
Execute a single turn of conversation.

Takes a user message, potentially executes multiple tool calls,
and returns the final response.

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.name: turn_stream
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        session: &mut Session,
        user_message: &str,
        cancellation: CancellationToken,
        workstream_id: Option<&str>,
    ) -> AgentStream
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment:
Execute a single turn of conversation with streaming output.

Returns a stream of chunks that yield text deltas, tool execution events,
and completion notifications as they occur.

# Arguments
* `session` - The session to operate on
* `user_message` - The user's message
* `cancellation` - Token to cancel the operation

# Returns
A stream of `StreamChunk` items

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.name: perform_recall
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment:
Perform active recall for a user message.

Embeds the user message, queries the memory store, and returns
a system message with relevant context if any matches are found.
Returns `None` if recall is disabled, not configured, or finds nothing.

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.name: with_max_total_tokens
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment:
Set cumulative token budget (input + output).

When set, the agent stops gracefully after exceeding this token total.
Useful as a safety valve for sub-agents like the RLM exploration agent.

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.name: with_workspace
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment:
Set the workspace path.

The workspace is the root directory for file operations.

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.name: with_prompt_builder
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment:
Set a prompt builder for dynamic system prompt generation.

When set, the builder will be used to generate the system prompt
at build time, incorporating tools and other context.

This takes precedence over `with_system_prompt()`.

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.name: with_bootstrap_dir
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment:
Load bootstrap context files from a directory.

Looks for BEHAVIOR.md, BOOTSTRAP.md, MEMORY.md, IDENTITY.md in the
specified directory and adds them to the prompt.

Can be combined with `with_prompt_file()` to add additional custom files.

# Example
```rust,ignore
let agent = Agent::builder()
.with_backend(backend)
.with_bootstrap_dir("/path/to/prompts")
.build()?;
```

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.name: with_prompt_file
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment:
Load a custom prompt file and add it to the bootstrap context.

Use this for prompt files with non-standard names. Can be called
multiple times to add multiple files. The file content will be
added to the bootstrap context section of the prompt.

# Example
```rust,ignore
let agent = Agent::builder()
.with_backend(backend)
.with_prompt_file("/path/to/custom_persona.md")
.with_prompt_file("/path/to/guidelines.md")
.build()?;
```

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment: Set the embedder for active recall.

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.name: with_recall_config

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.name: with_interaction_logger
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment: Set the interaction logger for structured JSONL capture.

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.name: with_plugin_prompts
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment:
Add plugin prompt fragments to the system prompt.

Each fragment is a `(plugin_name, prompt_text)` pair that will be
appended as a `## Plugin: {name}` section in the system prompt.

crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.name: with_hook_dispatcher
crates/arawn-agent/src/agent.rs.doc_comment:
Set the hook dispatcher for plugin lifecycle events.

The hook dispatcher fires hooks at lifecycle events like PreToolUse,
PostToolUse, SessionStart, and SessionEnd. PreToolUse hooks can block
tool execution.

Accepts any type implementing `HookDispatch`, wrapped in an Arc.

Builds a facts-only extraction prompt for hybrid mode.

When a local NER engine has already extracted entities and relationships,
the LLM only needs to extract facts. The NER entities are provided as
context so the LLM can generate higher-quality fact subjects.

crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/extracti.name: parse_extraction
crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/extracti.doc_comment:
Parse LLM output into an ExtractionResult.

Handles common failure modes:
- JSON wrapped in markdown code fences
- Trailing commas or minor syntax issues
- Partial/malformed output (returns what we can parse)

crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/gliner.r.name: GlinerEngine
crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/gliner.r.doc_comment:
GLiNER-based NER engine using span mode.

Wraps a `GLiNER<SpanMode>` model behind a Mutex since inference
requires `&mut self`.

crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/gliner.r.name: GlinerEngine
crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/gliner.r.doc_comment:
This module is only compiled when the `gliner` feature is enabled.

Concrete NER engine implementation using GLiNER via gline-rs.

crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/indexer..name: with_ner_engine
crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/indexer..doc_comment:
Set a local NER engine for hybrid extraction.

When set, entity and relationship extraction uses the local NER engine
instead of the LLM, and the LLM is only used for fact extraction.

crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/indexer..name: index_session
crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/indexer..doc_comment:
Run the full indexing pipeline for a session.

`session_id` identifies the session being indexed.
`messages` is the conversation history as `(role, content)` pairs.

Individual pipeline steps are best-effort: errors are logged in the
report but don't abort the remaining steps.

crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/ner.rs.name: NerEngine
crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/ner.rs.signature: { fn extract(), fn supports_relations(), fn extract_relations() }
crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/ner.rs.doc_comment:
Trait for local NER inference engines.

Implementations wrap specific NER backends (e.g., GLiNER via gline-rs)
and provide entity/relationship extraction without LLM calls.

crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/ner.rs.name: extract_relations
crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/ner.rs.doc_comment:
Run relation extraction on the given texts.

Default implementation returns empty results.
Engines that support relations should override this.

crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/summariz.name: build
crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/summariz.doc_comment:
Format a conversation history into a summarization prompt.

`messages` is a slice of `(role, content)` pairs representing the conversation.
Returns `None` if the conversation is empty (no summary needed).

crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/summariz.name: clean_summary
crates/arawn-agent/src/indexing/summariz.doc_comment:
Clean up LLM summary output by stripping common wrapper patterns.

Handles:
- Leading "Summary:" or "## Summary" labels
- Markdown code fences
- Excessive whitespace

crates/arawn-agent/src/lib.rs.name: agent
crates/arawn-agent/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
This crate provides the agent loop, tool framework, and task execution
capabilities that power Arawn's conversational AI features.

# Architecture

```text
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Agent                                                      │
│  - Orchestrates conversation loop                          │
│  - Manages tool execution                                   │
│  - Handles context building                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
│
┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
▼               ▼               ▼
┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐
│ LlmClient│    │ToolReg   │    │ Session  │
│(arawn-llm)│    │          │    │          │
└──────────┘    └──────────┘    └──────────┘
```

# Core Components

- [`Session`]: Conversation state container with turns
- [`Turn`]: Single exchange (user message + agent response)
- [`AgentConfig`]: Runtime configuration for the agent
- [`AgentResponse`]: Output from an agent turn

This crate provides the agent loop, tool framework, and task execution

Agent core for Arawn.

crates/arawn-agent/src/lib.rs.name: stream
crates/arawn-agent/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
- [`AgentResponse`]: Output from an agent turn
- [`AgentConfig`]: Runtime configuration for the agent
- [`Turn`]: Single exchange (user message + agent response)
- [`Session`]: Conversation state container with turns

# Core Components

```
└──────────┘    └──────────┘    └──────────┘
│(arawn-llm)│    │          │    │          │
│ LlmClient│    │ToolReg   │    │ Session  │
┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐
▼               ▼               ▼
┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
│
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
│  - Handles context building                                 │
│  - Manages tool execution                                   │
│  - Orchestrates conversation loop                          │
│  Agent                                                      │
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
```text

crates/arawn-agent/src/mcp.rs.signature: { full_name: String, server_name: String, tool_name: String, description: String, parameters: Value, client: Arc<McpClie...
crates/arawn-agent/src/mcp.rs.doc_comment:
Adapter that wraps an MCP tool as an Arawn [`Tool`].

This adapter:
- Maps MCP tool schemas to Arawn's JSON Schema format
- Delegates execution to the underlying [`McpClient`]
- Handles MCP error responses gracefully
- Supports tool namespacing (e.g., `mcp:sqlite:query`)

crates/arawn-agent/src/mcp.rs.name: McpToolAdapter
new
crates/arawn-agent/src/mcp.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new MCP tool adapter.

# Arguments
* `client` - Shared reference to the MCP client
* `tool_info` - Tool information from the MCP server

crates/arawn-agent/src/mcp.rs.name: from_client
crates/arawn-agent/src/mcp.rs.doc_comment:
Create adapters for all tools available from an MCP client.

This is a convenience method that calls `list_tools()` on the client
and creates an adapter for each tool.

# Errors
Returns an error if listing tools fails.

Check if a tool name matches this adapter's namespaced name.

Supports matching by:
- Full name: `mcp:sqlite:query`
- Server-qualified: `sqlite:query`
- Original name: `query` (only if unambiguous)

crates/arawn-agent/src/mcp.rs.name: parse_namespaced_name
crates/arawn-agent/src/mcp.rs.doc_comment:
Parse a namespaced tool name into its components.

# Returns
A tuple of (prefix, server_name, tool_name) if the name is fully qualified,
or None if the format is invalid.

# Examples
```rust,ignore
assert_eq!(
parse_namespaced_name("mcp:sqlite:query"),
Some(("mcp", "sqlite", "query"))
);
```

crates/arawn-agent/src/orchestrator.rs.doc_comment:
Create an orchestrator with a compaction backend that may differ from the agent's.

This allows using a cheaper/faster model for compaction while using
a more capable model for exploration.

crates/arawn-agent/src/orchestrator.rs.name: run
crates/arawn-agent/src/orchestrator.rs.doc_comment:
Run the agent with compaction-managed context.

The agent processes the query, calling tools as needed. When context
grows beyond the threshold, it's compacted and the agent continues.
The run stops when:
- The agent produces a response without tool calls (done)
- The agent's token budget is exceeded (truncated)
- The maximum compaction limit is reached (truncated)

crates/arawn-agent/src/prompt/bootstrap..name: load
crates/arawn-agent/src/prompt/bootstrap..doc_comment:
Load bootstrap files from a workspace directory.

Looks for standard bootstrap files (BEHAVIOR.md, BOOTSTRAP.md, etc.)
in the given directory and loads them with default options.

A BootstrapContext containing any files found. Returns an empty
context if the directory doesn't exist or contains no bootstrap files.

crates/arawn-agent/src/prompt/builder.rs.name: SystemPromptBuilder
crates/arawn-agent/src/prompt/builder.rs.signature: { mode: PromptMode, identity: Option<(String, String)>, tools: Option<Vec<ToolSummary>>, workspace_path: Option<PathBuf>...
crates/arawn-agent/src/prompt/builder.rs.doc_comment:
Builder for generating system prompts.

Assembles prompts from modular sections based on configuration.
Sections are joined with double newlines.

# Example

```rust,ignore
let prompt = SystemPromptBuilder::new()
.with_identity("Arawn", "A helpful AI assistant")
.with_tools(&registry)
.with_workspace("/project")
.build();
```

crates/arawn-agent/src/prompt/mod.rs.doc_comment:
This module provides a modular system for building agent system prompts.
Prompts are assembled from configurable sections including identity, tools,
workspace context, and bootstrap files.

# Example

```rust,ignore
use arawn_agent::prompt::{SystemPromptBuilder, PromptMode};

let prompt = SystemPromptBuilder::new()
.with_mode(PromptMode::Full)
.with_identity("Arawn", "A helpful AI assistant")
.with_tools(&tool_registry)
.with_workspace("/path/to/project")
.with_datetime(None)
.build();
```

crates/arawn-agent/src/prompt/mode.rs.name: PromptMode
crates/arawn-agent/src/prompt/mode.rs.doc_comment:
Mode controlling prompt verbosity and sections.

Different modes are appropriate for different agent contexts:
- `Full` for main conversational agents with all capabilities
- `Minimal` for subagents or task-focused agents
- `Identity` for lightweight agents that just need an identity line

crates/arawn-agent/src/rlm/mod.rs.name: prompt
crates/arawn-agent/src/rlm/mod.rs.doc_comment:
The RLM module provides an isolated sub-agent that explores information
spaces and returns compressed findings. It ties together:

- [`ToolRegistry::filtered_by_names`] for read-only tool access
- [`CompactionOrchestrator`] for iterative explore→compact→continue cycles
- A research-focused system prompt

# Usage

```rust,ignore
let spawner = RlmSpawner::new(backend, tool_registry);
let result = spawner.explore("How does authentication work?").await?;
println!("{}", result.summary);
```

crates/arawn-agent/src/rlm/mod.rs.name: types
crates/arawn-agent/src/rlm/mod.rs.doc_comment:
```
println!("{}", result.summary);
let result = spawner.explore("How does authentication work?").await?;
let spawner = RlmSpawner::new(backend, tool_registry);
```rust,ignore

# Usage

crates/arawn-agent/src/rlm/mod.rs.name: DEFAULT_READ_ONLY_TOOLS
crates/arawn-agent/src/rlm/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Default set of read-only tool names available to the RLM agent.

These tools allow exploration without side effects. Notably, `shell`,
`file_write`, `delegate`, and `note` are excluded to prevent mutations
and recursive sub-agent spawning.

crates/arawn-agent/src/rlm/mod.rs.name: RlmSpawner
crates/arawn-agent/src/rlm/mod.rs.signature: { backend: SharedBackend, compaction_backend: Option<SharedBackend>, tools: ToolRegistry, config: RlmConfig }
crates/arawn-agent/src/rlm/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Spawns isolated RLM exploration agents.

The spawner holds shared resources (backend, tools) and creates a fresh
agent + orchestrator for each [`explore`](RlmSpawner::explore) call.
This ensures each exploration is independent with its own session.

System prompt that instructs the agent to behave as a research explorer.

The RLM agent focuses on:
- Thorough exploration using available read-only tools
- Summarizing findings with citations to sources
- Building on compacted context from previous exploration cycles

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/command_vali.name: CommandValidator
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/command_vali.doc_comment:
Validates shell commands before execution as a defense-in-depth layer.

This validator runs **before** OS-level sandbox enforcement. The sandbox is
the primary security boundary; this is an additional check to catch clearly
dangerous or destructive commands early with a clear error message.

Uses regex patterns for precise matching — e.g. `rm -rf /` blocks root
deletion but allows `rm -rf /tmp/build`.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/command_vali.name: CommandValidation

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/command_vali.name: validate
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/command_vali.doc_comment:
Validate a shell command.

Returns `Allowed` if the command passes all checks, or `Blocked` with
a human-readable reason if it should be rejected.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/command_vali.name: normalize
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/command_vali.doc_comment:
Normalize a command for pattern matching.

Lowercases to defeat trivial blocklist bypasses like `RM -RF /`.
Whitespace is preserved for regex `\s+` matching.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/context.rs.name: sanitize
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/context.rs.doc_comment:
Sanitize this result according to the given configuration.

This method:
- Enforces size limits with truncation
- Strips null bytes and control characters (except newlines, tabs)
- Detects and rejects binary content
- Validates JSON structure for JSON results

Returns a sanitized result, or converts to an error if sanitization fails.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/execution.rs.name: execute
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/execution.rs.doc_comment:
Execute a tool by name.

The result is automatically sanitized with default configuration.

# Examples

```rust,ignore
let result = registry
.execute("read_file", serde_json::json!({"path": "/tmp/f.txt"}), &ctx)
.await?;
println!("{}", result.to_llm_content());
```

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/execution.rs.name: execute_with_config
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/execution.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        name: &str,
        params: serde_json::Value,
        ctx: &ToolContext,
        output_config: &OutputConfig,
    ) -> Result<ToolResult>
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/execution.rs.doc_comment:
Execute a tool by name with custom output configuration.

If a secret resolver is present on the context, `${{secrets.*}}` handles
in tool parameters are resolved to their real values before execution.
The caller retains the original params (with handles) for logging.

If a filesystem gate is present on the context, gated tools (file_read,
file_write, glob, grep, shell) are validated against workstream boundaries
before execution. If no gate is present and the tool is gated, execution
is denied by default.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/execution.rs.name: execute_raw
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/execution.rs.doc_comment:
Execute a tool by name without sanitization.

Secret handle resolution and filesystem gate enforcement still apply
even when output sanitization is skipped.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/execution.rs.name: resolve_secret_handles
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/execution.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve `${{secrets.*}}` handles in tool parameters.

If a secret resolver is present on the context and the params contain
handle references, replaces them with actual secret values. The original
params (with handles, not values) are what get logged by the caller.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/output.rs.doc_comment:
Sanitize a string according to the output configuration.

This function:
1. Detects and rejects binary content
2. Strips null bytes if configured
3. Strips control characters (except newlines, tabs) if configured
4. Truncates to max size if needed

Returns the sanitized string and whether it was truncated.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/registry.rs.doc_comment:
Registry for managing available tools.

The registry maintains a collection of tools that can be used by the agent.
It provides lookup by name and conversion to LLM tool definitions.

set_output_config
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/registry.rs.signature: (&mut self, name: impl Into<String>, config: OutputConfig)
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/registry.rs.doc_comment:
Set a per-tool output config override.

This override takes precedence over the hardcoded defaults in
`output_config_for()`. Multiple tool names can map to the same
config (e.g., "shell" and "bash" share a limit).

crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/registry.rs.name: filtered_by_names
crates/arawn-agent/src/tool/registry.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new registry containing only tools whose names are in the allowlist.

Returns a new `ToolRegistry` with cloned `Arc` refs for matching tools.
Names not matching any registered tool are silently ignored.
Output config overrides for matching tools are also carried over.

Checks user-configured overrides first, then falls back to
hardcoded per-tool defaults.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tools/delegate.rs.name: DelegateTool
crates/arawn-agent/src/tools/delegate.rs.doc_comment:
Tool for delegating tasks to subagents.

Subagents are specialized agents with constrained tool sets, defined
in plugin configurations. The delegate tool allows the main agent to
invoke these subagents for specialized tasks.

# Example Usage

```json
{
"agent": "researcher",
"task": "Find recent papers on RAG architectures",
"context": "The user is building a knowledge retrieval system",
"background": false
}
```

crates/arawn-agent/src/tools/delegate.rs.name: DelegateTool

Allows the main agent to delegate tasks to specialized subagents

crates/arawn-agent/src/tools/explore.rs.name: ExploreTool
crates/arawn-agent/src/tools/explore.rs.doc_comment:
Tool that spawns an RLM exploration agent to research a query.

The exploration agent has read-only access to tools (file_read, grep, glob,
web_search, etc.) and uses iterative compaction to work beyond context
limits. Results are returned as a summary with metadata.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tools/explore.rs.name: ExploreTool
crates/arawn-agent/src/tools/explore.rs.doc_comment:
delegate research tasks to an isolated exploration sub-agent.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tools/file.rs.name: reject_traversal
crates/arawn-agent/src/tools/file.rs.doc_comment:
Reject paths that contain `..` (parent directory) traversal components.

This is a defense-in-depth check. In the normal flow, the FsGate
canonicalizes paths before they reach the tool, so legitimate paths
never contain `..` sequences. Any remaining traversal indicates a
bypass attempt.

crates/arawn-agent/src/tools/file.rs.name: normalize_path
crates/arawn-agent/src/tools/file.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve `..` and `.` components lexically (without filesystem access).

Used as a fallback when the filesystem path doesn't exist yet and
`canonicalize()` can't be called.

- Stored facts and knowledge
- User preferences and information
- Research findings and notes

crates/arawn-agent/src/types.rs.name: set_context_preamble
crates/arawn-agent/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Set a context preamble that's included in system prompts but not in turn history.

Use this to inject dynamic context (e.g., from a parent agent) that the LLM
should see but that shouldn't become part of the conversation record.

# Example

```rust,ignore
let mut session = Session::new();
session.set_context_preamble("[Context from parent]\nUser is researching RAG systems.");
```

crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment: Default config filename within XDG config directory.

crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.name: APP_NAME
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment: Application name for XDG directory resolution.

crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.name: ConfigSource
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment: Tracks where each config layer was loaded from.

crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.name: LoadedConfig
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.signature: { config: ArawnConfig, sources: Vec<ConfigSource>, source: Option<ConfigSource>, warnings: Vec<String> }
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment: Result of config discovery and loading.

crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.name: LoadedConfig
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment:
3. CLI arguments (handled externally)
2. `./arawn.toml` (project-local)
1. `~/.config/arawn/config.toml` (XDG user config)
Resolution order (later overrides earlier):

Config file discovery and layered merging.

crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.name: loaded_from
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment: Get paths of sources that were actually loaded.

crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.name: load_config
load_config_with_options
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment:
Load configuration with explicit control over the user config directory.

`config_dir` overrides both `ARAWN_CONFIG_DIR` and the platform default.
Pass `Some(path)` to use a specific directory, or `None` to use the default resolution.

crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.name: load_config_file
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment: Load config from a specific file path (no discovery).

crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.name: save_config
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment:
Save configuration to a file.

Creates parent directories if they don't exist.

crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.name: CONFIG_DIR_ENV
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment:
Environment variable to override the config directory.

When set, this takes precedence over the platform default (XDG/Application Support).
Useful for testing and running multiple instances with different configs.

crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.name: xdg_config_path
xdg_config_dir
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment:
Get the XDG config directory for arawn.

Checks `ARAWN_CONFIG_DIR` env var first, then falls back to platform default.

) -> Result<ConfigSource>
crates/arawn-config/src/discovery.rs.doc_comment: Try to load a config file and merge it into the existing config.

crates/arawn-config/src/lib.rs.name: age_crypto
crates/arawn-config/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Provides TOML-based configuration with:
- Named LLM configurations (`[llm]`, `[llm.claude]`, `[llm.fast]`, etc.)
- Cascading agent-to-LLM binding with fallback defaults
- Config file layering (XDG user config + project-local overrides)
- API key resolution (age store → env var → config file)

Client configuration (kubeconfig-style):
- Named connection contexts (server + auth)
- `current-context` for default selection
- Multiple auth methods (API key, OAuth, bearer)

See ADR ARAWN-A-0001 for architectural decisions.

crates/arawn-config/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:

- Cascading agent-to-LLM binding with fallback defaults
- Named LLM configurations (`[llm]`, `[llm.claude]`, `[llm.fast]`, etc.)

Provides TOML-based configuration with:

Configuration system for the Arawn agent framework.

crates/arawn-config/src/lib.rs.name: types
crates/arawn-config/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
See ADR ARAWN-A-0001 for architectural decisions.

- Multiple auth methods (API key, OAuth, bearer)
- `current-context` for default selection
- Named connection contexts (server + auth)
Client configuration (kubeconfig-style):

- API key resolution (age store → env var → config file)
- Config file layering (XDG user config + project-local overrides)
- Cascading agent-to-LLM binding with fallback defaults
- Named LLM configurations (`[llm]`, `[llm.claude]`, `[llm.fast]`, etc.)
Provides TOML-based configuration with:

Configuration system for the Arawn agent framework.

crates/arawn-config/src/resolver.rs.name: ResolvedLlm
crates/arawn-config/src/resolver.rs.signature: { backend: Backend, model: String, base_url: Option<String>, api_key: Option<String>, api_key_source: Option<SecretSourc...
crates/arawn-config/src/resolver.rs.doc_comment:
A fully resolved LLM configuration ready to construct a backend.

The `Debug` impl intentionally redacts `api_key` to prevent secret leakage
in log output. Use `.api_key` directly when the actual key is needed.

# Examples

```rust,ignore
let config = ArawnConfig::from_toml(r#"
[llm]
backend = "anthropic"
model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
"#)?;
let resolved = arawn_config::resolve_for_agent(&config, "default")?;
assert_eq!(resolved.model, "claude-sonnet-4-20250514");
println!("{:?}", resolved); // api_key is redacted in Debug output
```

crates/arawn-config/src/resolver.rs.name: ResolvedLlm
crates/arawn-config/src/resolver.rs.doc_comment:
a given agent, handling cascading defaults and API key lookup.
Takes an [`ArawnConfig`] and resolves a fully-qualified [`ResolvedLlm`] for

LLM config resolution — turns named config references into concrete backend configs.

crates/arawn-config/src/resolver.rs.name: ApiKeySource
crates/arawn-config/src/resolver.rs.doc_comment: How an API key was resolved.

crates/arawn-config/src/resolver.rs.name: resolve_for_agent
crates/arawn-config/src/resolver.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve the LLM config for a given agent name.

This performs the full cascading resolution and API key lookup:
1. Resolve which `LlmConfig` applies (agent-specific → agent.default → global)
2. Validate required fields (backend, model)
3. Resolve API key (keyring → env var → config file)

# Examples

```rust,ignore
let config = arawn_config::load_config(None)?.config;
let resolved = arawn_config::resolve_for_agent(&config, "summarizer")?;
println!("Using {} on {}", resolved.backend, resolved.model);
```

    agent_name: &str,
) -> Result<(&'a LlmConfig, ResolvedFrom)>

crates/arawn-config/src/secret_store.rs.name: AgeSecretStore
crates/arawn-config/src/secret_store.rs.signature: { identity: age::x25519::Identity, secrets_path: PathBuf, cache: RwLock<BTreeMap<String, String>> }
crates/arawn-config/src/secret_store.rs.doc_comment:
An age-encrypted secret store.

Secrets are stored as a JSON map `{ "name": "value", ... }` encrypted
with the user's age identity. The decrypted map is cached in memory
and written back on mutation.

crates/arawn-config/src/secret_store.rs.name: AgeSecretStore

be injected into the agent's `ToolContext` for handle resolution.

The encrypted file lives at `~/.config/arawn/secrets.age`.
Stores secrets as a JSON map encrypted with an age identity.

Age-encrypted secret store.

crates/arawn-config/src/secret_store.rs.name: open
crates/arawn-config/src/secret_store.rs.doc_comment:
Open or create a secret store.

Loads the age identity from `identity_path` (generating one if needed),
then decrypts the secrets file at `secrets_path` (creating empty if needed).

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.signature: { llm: Option<LlmConfig>, llm_profiles: HashMap<String, LlmConfig>, agent: HashMap<String, AgentProfileConfig>, server: ...
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Root configuration structure.

Maps to the full TOML config file. All sections are optional so that
partial configs (e.g., project-local overrides) can be loaded and merged.

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: ArawnConfig
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
```
[server]                 # server settings
[agent.summarizer]       # per-agent overrides
[agent.default]          # default agent settings
[llm.claude]             # named LLM configs
[llm]                    # default LLM
```toml
Top-level config:

Configuration types mapping to the TOML schema.

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: new
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment: Create an empty config.

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: from_toml
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Parse from a TOML string.

# Examples

```rust,ignore
let config = ArawnConfig::from_toml(r#"
[llm]
backend = "anthropic"
model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
"#)?;
assert!(config.llm.is_some());
```

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: to_toml
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment: Serialize to a TOML string.

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: merge
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Merge another config on top of this one (other takes priority).

# Examples

```rust,ignore
let mut base = ArawnConfig::from_toml(r#"
[llm]
backend = "groq"
model = "llama-3.1-70b-versatile"
"#)?;
let overlay = ArawnConfig::from_toml(r#"
[server]
port = 9090
"#)?;
base.merge(overlay);
assert_eq!(base.server.unwrap().port, 9090);
```

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: resolve_llm
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve the LLM config for a given agent name.

Resolution order:
1. `agent.<name>.llm` → lookup in `llm_profiles`
2. `agent.default.llm` → lookup in `llm_profiles`
3. `[llm]` (global default)

# Examples

```rust,ignore
let config = ArawnConfig::from_toml(r#"
[llm]
backend = "groq"
model = "llama-3.1-70b-versatile"

[llm.claude]
backend = "anthropic"
model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"

[agent.default]
llm = "claude"
"#)?;
let llm = config.resolve_llm("researcher")?;
assert_eq!(llm.model.as_deref(), Some("claude-sonnet-4-20250514"));
```

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.signature: { llm: Option<RawLlmSection>, agent: HashMap<String, AgentProfileConfig>, server: Option<ServerConfig>, logging: Option<...
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Internal raw config matching the actual TOML layout.

In TOML, `[llm]` and `[llm.claude]` coexist as a table with both
direct keys and sub-tables. This struct handles that mapping.

indexing. Default is local ONNX inference (offline-first, zero-config).

[embedding.openai]

```

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: DelegationConfig
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Subagent delegation configuration.

Controls behavior for delegating tasks to subagents, including result
compaction (LLM-based summarization of long responses).

```toml
[delegation]
max_result_len = 8000

[delegation.compaction]
enabled = true
threshold = 8000
backend = "default"
model = "gpt-4o-mini"
```

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: McpConfig
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
MCP (Model Context Protocol) configuration.

Configures MCP servers that provide external tools to the agent.
Servers can be configured globally or per-plugin.

```toml
[mcp]
enabled = true

[[mcp.servers]]
name = "sqlite"
command = "mcp-server-sqlite"
args = ["--db", "/path/to/db.sqlite"]

[[mcp.servers]]
name = "filesystem"
command = "mcp-server-filesystem"
args = ["--allowed-dirs", "/home/user/projects"]
env = [["DEBUG", "1"]]
```

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: CompressionConfig
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.signature: { enabled: bool, backend: String, model: String, max_summary_tokens: u32, token_threshold_chars: usize }
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Configuration for automatic session/workstream compression.

When enabled, sessions are automatically compressed via LLM summarization
when they end, and workstream summaries are updated from session summaries.

```toml
[workstream.compression]
enabled = true
backend = "default"
model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
max_summary_tokens = 1024
token_threshold_chars = 32000
```

Controls timeouts and output limits for various tool types.

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: ToolOutputConfig
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.signature: { max_size_bytes: usize, shell: Option<usize>, file_read: Option<usize>, web_fetch: Option<usize>, search: Option<usize>...
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Tool output configuration.

Per-tool limits override the global `max_size_bytes` default.
When a per-tool value is `None`, the hardcoded default for that
tool type is used (shell=100KB, file_read=500KB, web_fetch=200KB,
search=50KB).

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: RlmTomlConfig
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.signature: { model: Option<String>, max_turns: Option<u32>, max_context_tokens: Option<usize>, compaction_threshold: Option<f32>, m...
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Configuration for the RLM (Recursive Language Model) exploration agent.

Maps to the `[rlm]` section in `arawn.toml`. All fields are optional;
when absent, the agent-side `RlmConfig` defaults apply.

```toml
[rlm]
model = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
max_turns = 25
max_context_tokens = 50000
compaction_threshold = 0.7
max_compactions = 10
compaction_model = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
```

crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.name: OAuthConfigOverride
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.signature: { client_id: Option<String>, authorize_url: Option<String>, token_url: Option<String>, redirect_uri: Option<String>, sco...
crates/arawn-config/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
OAuth configuration overrides for the `[oauth]` TOML section.

All fields are optional — any field not set falls through to environment
variables and then built-in defaults in `arawn-oauth`.
# Examples

```rust,ignore
let config = ArawnConfig::from_toml(r#"
[oauth]
client_id = "my-client-id"
scope = "openid profile"
"#)?;
let oauth = config.oauth.unwrap();
assert_eq!(oauth.client_id.as_deref(), Some("my-client-id"));
```

crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.doc_comment:
Execute an async operation with exponential backoff retry.

Retries on transient errors (network failures) and rate limits.
For rate limits, uses provider-specified retry delay when available.
Non-retryable errors are returned immediately.

crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.name: supports_native_tools
crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.doc_comment:
Returns true if backend handles tools natively via API.

When true:
- Tools are passed via `request.tools` to the API
- Responses contain structured `tool_use` content blocks
- No prompt injection or output parsing needed

When false (default):
- Tools are formatted and injected into the system prompt
- Responses must be parsed for tool calls

crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.name: tool_calling_instructions
crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.doc_comment:
Instructions for HOW to call tools (model-specific format).

This is appended to the system prompt to tell the model the expected
output format for tool calls. Only used when `supports_native_tools()` is false.

Returns None if no special instructions needed (uses default human-readable).

crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.name: format_tool_definitions
crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.doc_comment:
Format tool definitions for the system prompt.

Converts tool definitions into a string that's injected into the system
prompt. Only used when `supports_native_tools()` is false.

Default: human-readable format with parameters.

crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.name: format_tool_result
crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.doc_comment:
Format a tool result for the conversation.

Converts tool execution results into the format expected by the model.
Only used when `supports_native_tools()` is false.

Default: simple text format.

crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.name: parse_tool_calls
crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.doc_comment:
Parse tool calls from model text output.

Extracts tool calls from the model's response text and returns the
remaining text along with parsed tool calls.
Only used when `supports_native_tools()` is false.

Default: no parsing, returns original text with empty tool calls.

crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.name: default_format_tool_definitions
crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.doc_comment: Default human-readable format for tool definitions.

crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.name: default_format_tool_result
crates/arawn-llm/src/backend.rs.doc_comment: Default format for tool results.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.signature: { fn embed(), fn embed_batch(), fn dimensions(), fn name() }
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment:
Trait for generating text embeddings.

Embedders convert text into dense vector representations that capture
semantic meaning, enabling similarity search and retrieval.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: embed_batch
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment:
Generate embeddings for multiple texts in a batch.

Default implementation calls `embed` for each text sequentially.
Implementations may override for more efficient batching.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: SharedEmbedder
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment: A shared embedder that can be used across threads.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: MockEmbedder
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment:
A mock embedder for testing purposes.

Generates deterministic embeddings based on text content, useful for
testing similarity search and memory retrieval without external dependencies.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: MockEmbedder
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment:
- [`OpenAiEmbedder`]: Uses OpenAI's embeddings API
- [`LocalEmbedder`]: Uses ONNX Runtime for local inference (requires `local-embeddings` feature)
- [`MockEmbedder`]: Returns deterministic embeddings for testing

# Implementations

- Context retrieval for conversations
- Finding similar content
- Semantic search in the memory store
generating vector embeddings from text. Embeddings are used for:
This module provides the [`Embedder`] trait and implementations for

Embeddings support for semantic search and memory.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: LocalEmbedder
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.signature: { session: Mutex<Session>, tokenizer: Tokenizer, dimensions: usize, name: String }
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment:
Local embedder using ONNX Runtime.

This embedder runs inference locally using an ONNX model, enabling
offline operation and avoiding API costs.

The session is wrapped in a Mutex because ONNX Runtime's `run()` requires
mutable access, but the Embedder trait uses `&self`.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.signature:
(
            model_path: impl AsRef<Path>,
            tokenizer_path: impl AsRef<Path>,
            dimensions: usize,
        ) -> Result<Self>
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment:
Load a local embedder from model files.

# Arguments
* `model_path` - Path to the ONNX model file
* `tokenizer_path` - Path to the tokenizer.json file
* `dimensions` - Output embedding dimensions

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: run_batch
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment:
Run ONNX inference on a batch of encodings.

Pads all sequences to the same length and runs a single
`session.run()` call with batched tensors.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: EmbedderSpec
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.signature: { provider: String, openai_api_key: Option<String>, openai_model: Option<String>, openai_base_url: Option<String>, local...
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment:
Configuration for building an embedder from application config.

This is a provider-agnostic struct that `start.rs` populates from
`EmbeddingConfig`. It avoids a dependency from arawn-llm → arawn-config.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: build_embedder
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment:
Build a `SharedEmbedder` from a spec.

Falls back to `MockEmbedder` if the requested provider is unavailable
(e.g., "local" requested but `local-embeddings` feature disabled).

For local embeddings, this will automatically download model files
from HuggingFace if they don't exist (~22MB).

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: default_local_model_dir
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment: Default directory for local embedding model files.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL_URL
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment: Default HuggingFace model URL for all-MiniLM-L6-v2 ONNX model.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_TOKENIZER_URL
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment: Default HuggingFace tokenizer URL for all-MiniLM-L6-v2.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: DEFAULT_NER_MODEL_URL
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment: Default HuggingFace model URL for GLiNER small v2.1 (span mode).

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: DEFAULT_NER_TOKENIZER_URL
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment: Default HuggingFace tokenizer URL for GLiNER small v2.1.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: ensure_model_files
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment:
Download embedding model files if they don't exist.

Returns the directory path where files were downloaded, or None if download failed.
Accepts optional URL overrides; falls back to HuggingFace defaults.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: default_ner_model_dir
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment: Default directory for NER (GLiNER) model files.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: ensure_ner_model_files
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.signature:
(
    model_url: Option<&str>,
    tokenizer_url: Option<&str>,
) -> Option<(std::path::PathBuf, std::path::PathBuf)>
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment:
Download NER (GLiNER) model files if they don't exist.

Returns `(model_path, tokenizer_path)` or None if download failed.
Accepts optional URL overrides; falls back to HuggingFace defaults.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: download_file
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment: Download a file from URL to path.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: cosine_similarity
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment: Calculate cosine similarity between two embeddings.

crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.name: euclidean_distance
crates/arawn-llm/src/embeddings.rs.doc_comment: Calculate Euclidean distance between two embeddings.

crates/arawn-llm/src/lib.rs.name: backend
crates/arawn-llm/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
This crate provides a unified interface for interacting with various LLM providers
(Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama, etc.) with support for streaming responses and tool calling.

# Architecture

The core abstraction is the [`LlmBackend`] trait which all providers implement.
This allows the agent to use any provider interchangeably.

```text
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  LlmBackend trait                       │
│  - complete() -> Response               │
│  - complete_stream() -> Stream<Event>   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
│
┌──────────────┼──────────────┐
▼              ▼              ▼
┌────────┐   ┌──────────┐   ┌────────┐
│Anthropic│   │  OpenAI  │   │ Ollama │
└────────┘   └──────────┘   └────────┘
```

crates/arawn-llm/src/lib.rs.name: client
crates/arawn-llm/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
```
└────────┘   └──────────┘   └────────┘
│Anthropic│   │  OpenAI  │   │ Ollama │
┌────────┐   ┌──────────┐   ┌────────┐
▼              ▼              ▼
┌──────────────┼──────────────┐
│
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
│  - complete_stream() -> Stream<Event>   │
│  - complete() -> Response               │
│  LlmBackend trait                       │
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
```text

This allows the agent to use any provider interchangeably.
The core abstraction is the [`LlmBackend`] trait which all providers implement.

# Architecture

(Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama, etc.) with support for streaming responses and tool calling.

└────────┘   └──────────┘   └────────┘
│Anthropic│   │  OpenAI  │   │ Ollama │
┌────────┐   ┌──────────┐   ┌────────┐
▼              ▼              ▼
┌──────────────┼──────────────┐
│
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
│  - complete_stream() -> Stream<Event>   │
│  - complete() -> Response               │
│  LlmBackend trait                       │
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
```text

This allows the agent to use any provider interchangeably.
The core abstraction is the [`LlmBackend`] trait which all providers implement.

# Architecture

(Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama, etc.) with support for streaming responses and tool calling.
This crate provides a unified interface for interacting with various LLM providers

LLM client abstraction for Arawn.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/client.rs.name: connect
crates/arawn-mcp/src/client.rs.doc_comment:
Connect to an MCP server using the configured transport.

Automatically selects stdio or HTTP based on the config.
This does NOT initialize the connection - call `initialize()` after connecting.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/client.rs.name: connect_stdio
crates/arawn-mcp/src/client.rs.doc_comment:
Connect to an MCP server using stdio transport.

This spawns the server process but does NOT initialize the connection.
Call `initialize()` after connecting to complete the handshake.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/client.rs.name: connect_http
crates/arawn-mcp/src/client.rs.doc_comment:
Connect to an MCP server using HTTP transport.

This creates an HTTP client but does NOT initialize the connection.
Call `initialize()` after connecting to complete the handshake.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/client.rs.name: initialize
crates/arawn-mcp/src/client.rs.doc_comment:
Initialize the connection with the MCP server.

This performs the MCP handshake, exchanging capabilities and protocol versions.
Must be called before using other methods.

* `arguments` - The arguments to pass to the tool

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: McpManager
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.signature: { configs: HashMap<String, McpServerConfig>, clients: HashMap<String, Arc<McpClient>> }
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
Manager for multiple MCP server connections.

Provides lifecycle management for MCP servers, including:
- Adding and removing server configurations
- Connecting to and initializing all servers
- Discovering tools from all connected servers
- Graceful shutdown of all connections

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: McpManager
new
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment: Create a new empty MCP manager.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: with_configs
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment: Create a manager with the given server configurations.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: add_server
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
Add a server configuration.

The server will not be connected until [`connect_all`] is called.
If a server with the same name already exists, it will be replaced.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: remove_server
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
Remove a server by name.

If the server is connected, it will be disconnected first.
Returns true if the server was found and removed.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: server_names
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment: Get the names of all configured servers.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: connected_server_names
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment: Get the names of all connected servers.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: has_server
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment: Check if a server is configured.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: is_connected
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment: Check if a server is connected.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: get_client
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment: Get a connected client by name.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: connect_all
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
Connect to all configured servers.

For each configured server:
1. Spawns the server process
2. Performs the MCP initialization handshake
3. Stores the client for later use

Servers that fail to connect are logged and skipped.
Returns the number of successfully connected servers.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: connect_server
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment: Connect to a single server.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: connect_server_by_name
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
Connect a single server by name.

If the server is already connected, returns Ok without reconnecting.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: list_all_tools
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
List all tools from all connected servers.

Returns a map of server name to list of tools.

crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.name: all_tools_flat
crates/arawn-mcp/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:

crates/arawn-mcp/tests/mock_server.rs.doc_comment:
--slow-tool T:MS   Add MS delay when tool T is called
--crash-on TOOL    Exit with code 1 when TOOL is called
--delay-ms N       Add N ms delay to all responses
Options:

mock-mcp-server [--delay-ms N] [--crash-on TOOL] [--slow-tool TOOL:MS]
Usage:

This is a simple MCP server that responds to initialize, tools/list, and tools/call.

crates/arawn-memory/src/backend.rs.name: MemoryBackend
crates/arawn-memory/src/backend.rs.signature: { fn insert(), fn get(), fn update(), fn delete(), fn list(), fn count(), fn touch() }
crates/arawn-memory/src/backend.rs.doc_comment:
Trait for memory storage backends.

This trait defines the core operations for storing and retrieving memories.
Implementations can use different storage technologies (SQLite, Redis, etc.)
while providing a consistent interface.

# Thread Safety

All implementations must be `Send + Sync` to allow sharing across threads.

crates/arawn-memory/src/backend.rs.name: MemoryBackendExt
crates/arawn-memory/src/backend.rs.signature: { fn find_contradictions(), fn supersede(), fn reinforce(), fn update_last_accessed() }
crates/arawn-memory/src/backend.rs.doc_comment:
Extension trait for advanced memory operations.

These operations are optional and may not be supported by all backends.
The default implementations return `Ok(Vec::new())` or `Ok(())`.

crates/arawn-memory/src/backend.rs.name: find_contradictions

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/mod.rs.name: graph_ops
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Provides persistent storage for memories, sessions, and notes using rusqlite.
Integrates sqlite-vec for vector search and graphqlite for knowledge graphs.

# Unified API

The store provides both low-level operations (insert_memory, get_memory, etc.)
and a unified API for operations that span multiple subsystems:

- `store()`: Store a memory with optional embedding and graph entities
- `get_with_context()`: Retrieve a memory with its graph relationships
- `delete_cascade()`: Remove a memory and all associated data
- `update_indexed()`: Update a memory and re-index its embedding/entities

- `update_indexed()`: Update a memory and re-index its embedding/entities
- `delete_cascade()`: Remove a memory and all associated data
- `get_with_context()`: Retrieve a memory with its graph relationships
- `store()`: Store a memory with optional embedding and graph entities

and a unified API for operations that span multiple subsystems:
The store provides both low-level operations (insert_memory, get_memory, etc.)

# Unified API

Integrates sqlite-vec for vector search and graphqlite for knowledge graphs.
Provides persistent storage for memories, sessions, and notes using rusqlite.

Memory store implementation using SQLite.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/mod.rs.name: open

Creates the database file and initializes the schema if it doesn't exist.

use `init_graph_at_path`.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/recall.rs.name: recall
search_memories
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/recall.rs.doc_comment:
Simple text search across memories.

This is a fallback when vector search is not available or for
exact text matching.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/recall.rs.name: compute_staleness
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/recall.rs.doc_comment:
Compute staleness status for a memory based on its citation.

For file citations, checks if the file's mtime has changed.
For web citations, applies age-based staleness (7 days default).
Other citation types and missing citations return Unknown.

    ) -> Result<Vec<Memory>>

If a session with the given ID exists, it is returned.
Otherwise, a new session is created with that ID and returned.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/session_op.name: append_to_session
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/session_op.signature:
(
        &self,
        session_id: SessionId,
        content_type: ContentType,
        content: impl Into<String>,
    ) -> Result<MemoryId>
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/session_op.doc_comment:
Append an entry to a session.

Creates a Memory with the session_id in its metadata.
This allows session history to be searched via vector similarity.

Returns the created memory's ID.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/session_op.signature:
(

        content_type: ContentType,
        content: impl Into<String>,
        embedding: &[f32],
    ) -> Result<MemoryId>
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/session_op.doc_comment:
Append an entry to a session with an optional embedding.

Like `append_to_session`, but also stores the embedding for vector search.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/session_op.signature:
(

        limit: usize,
        offset: usize,
    ) -> Result<Vec<Memory>>
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/session_op.doc_comment:
Get session history (all memories associated with a session).

Returns memories ordered by creation time (oldest first).

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/session_op.name: row_to_session
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/session_op.doc_comment: Convert a database row to a Session struct.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/unified_op.name: MemoryStore
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/unified_op.doc_comment: Unified API: store, get_with_context, delete_cascade, update_indexed.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/unified_op.name: store
get_with_context
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/unified_op.doc_comment:
Retrieve a memory with its graph context.

Returns the memory along with related entities from the knowledge graph
and whether it has an embedding stored.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/unified_op.name: delete_cascade
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/unified_op.doc_comment:
Delete a memory and all associated data (cascade delete).

Removes:
- The memory from SQLite
- The embedding (if stored)
- The graph node and its relationships (if in graph)

Returns `true` if the memory was found and deleted.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/unified_op.name: update_indexed
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/unified_op.doc_comment:
Update a memory and re-index its embedding and entities.

This updates the memory content and optionally:
- Replaces the embedding
- Updates graph entities (removes old links, adds new ones)

Note: For entity updates, this removes all existing relationships
from this memory and creates new ones based on the provided entities.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/unified_op.name: store_fact
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/unified_op.doc_comment:
Store a fact with automatic reinforcement and contradiction detection.

If the memory's metadata contains `subject` and `predicate` fields:
1. If an existing memory has the same subject+predicate+content, reinforce it
(increment count, update last_accessed) and skip insertion.
2. Otherwise, supersede any existing memories with different content.

Returns a `StoreFactResult` describing what happened.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.name: init_vectors
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.doc_comment:
Initialize vector search capabilities.

This must be called before using any vector operations.
Creates the vector embeddings table if it doesn't exist.
Initialize vector storage with dimension mismatch detection.

Stores the embedding dimensions and provider name in the metadata table.
If previously stored dimensions differ from the requested dimensions,
vectors are marked as stale and search will return empty results until
a reindex is performed.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.name: vectors_stale
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.doc_comment: Check if vector embeddings are stale (dimension/provider mismatch).

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.name: reindex_dry_run
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.doc_comment: Dry-run reindex: returns counts without doing any work.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.name: reindex
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.signature:
(
        &self,
        embed_batch: F,
        new_dims: usize,
        new_provider: &str,
    ) -> Result<ReindexReport>
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.doc_comment:
Reindex all memory embeddings with a new embedder/dimensions.

Drops the existing vector table, recreates it with the new dimensions,
and re-embeds all memories using the provided embed function.

The `embed_batch` closure receives a batch of text strings and returns
their embeddings. This avoids coupling arawn-memory to arawn-llm.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.name: insert_memory_with_embedding
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.doc_comment:

This is a convenience method that inserts both the memory and its embedding
in a single operation.

crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.name: search_similar_memories
crates/arawn-memory/src/store/vector_ops.doc_comment:

crates/arawn-memory/src/types.rs.name: compute_score
crates/arawn-memory/src/types.rs.doc_comment:
Compute a composite confidence score from all factors.

Score formula:
1. Base score from source type
2. Multiply by reinforcement boost: `min(1.0 + 0.1 * count, cap)`
3. Multiply by staleness factor (1.0 for recent, decays linearly to floor)
4. Superseded memories always return 0.0

crates/arawn-memory/src/validation.rs.name: validate_embedding
crates/arawn-memory/src/validation.rs.doc_comment:
Validate an embedding vector.

Checks:
1. Dimension matches expected size
2. No NaN or Inf values

# Arguments
* `embedding` - The embedding vector to validate
* `expected_dim` - The expected dimension size

# Returns
Ok(()) if valid, Err(ValidationError) otherwise

Checks:
1. Content is not empty
2. Content is valid UTF-8 (already guaranteed by String type, but checked explicitly)

crates/arawn-oauth/src/lib.rs.name: error
crates/arawn-oauth/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Provides a vendored OAuth proxy that enables Arawn to use Claude MAX
subscription credits without a separate API key. Modeled after muninn's
proxy implementation.

# Components

- [`oauth`] — PKCE flow: challenge generation, authorization URL, token exchange/refresh
- [`token_manager`] — Token persistence and automatic refresh
- [`passthrough`] — Request mangling: system prompt injection, field stripping, auth headers
- [`proxy`] — Axum-based localhost proxy server

crates/arawn-oauth/src/lib.rs.name: oauth
crates/arawn-oauth/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
- [`proxy`] — Axum-based localhost proxy server
- [`passthrough`] — Request mangling: system prompt injection, field stripping, auth headers
- [`token_manager`] — Token persistence and automatic refresh
- [`oauth`] — PKCE flow: challenge generation, authorization URL, token exchange/refresh

# Components

custom/
http.wasm
passthrough.wasm
builtin/
catalog.toml

directories that hold `.wasm` modules.
The catalog persists as `catalog.toml` alongside `builtin/` and `custom/`

Runtime catalog for managing WASM runtime modules.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/catalog.rs.name: load
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/catalog.rs.doc_comment:
Load or initialize a catalog from the given runtimes directory.

Creates `builtin/` and `custom/` subdirectories if they don't exist.
If `catalog.toml` doesn't exist, starts with an empty catalog.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.name: ContextResolver
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.doc_comment: Resolves `{{expression}}` templates against a context data map.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.name: new
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.doc_comment:
Create a resolver backed by a context data map.

The map keys are top-level identifiers (task IDs, "input", etc.)
and values are the JSON data associated with each.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.name: resolve_value
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve all `{{...}}` templates in a JSON value tree.

- Strings: template expressions are replaced inline
- Objects/Arrays: recursively resolved
- Other types: returned unchanged

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.name: resolve_string
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve all `{{...}}` templates in a string.

If the entire string is a single template expression (e.g., `"{{task.output}}"`)
the result preserves the original JSON type (object, array, number, etc.).

If the string contains mixed text and templates (e.g., `"Hello {{name}}"`),
all expressions are stringified and concatenated.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.name: resolve_expression
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.doc_comment: Resolve a single dot-separated path expression against the context.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.name: TemplateExpression
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.doc_comment: A parsed `{{expression}}` occurrence in a string.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.name: parse_template_expressions
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.doc_comment: Find all `{{...}}` expressions in a string.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.name: PathSegment
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.doc_comment: A segment of a dot-separated path, optionally with an array index.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.name: PathSegment
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.doc_comment:
LLM prompts, or tool parameters. Multiple templates per string are supported.
Templates can appear anywhere in string values within action params,

- `{{task_id.output.items[0].name}}` — array index access
- `{{task_id.output.field}}` — nested field access via dot notation
- `{{task_id.output}}` — full output of a completed task
- `{{input.field}}` — access workflow-level input context

# Template Syntax

context, enabling upstream task outputs to feed into downstream inputs.
Resolves `{{expression}}` templates in task parameters against the workflow

Context template resolver for workflow data flow.

Parse a dot-separated path into segments, handling array indices.

`"task.output.items[0].name"` → `[("task", None), ("output", None), ("items", Some(0)), ("name", None)]`

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.name: navigate_segment
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.doc_comment: Navigate one segment of a path through a JSON value.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.name: value_to_string
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/context.rs.doc_comment: Convert a JSON value to its string representation for template interpolation.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.name: WorkflowFile
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.doc_comment: Top-level wrapper matching the TOML structure `[workflow]`.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.name: WorkflowDefinition
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.signature: { name: String, description: String, tasks: Vec<TaskDefinition>, schedule: Option<ScheduleConfig>, runtime: Option<Runti...

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.name: TaskDefinition
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.signature: { id: String, action: Option<ActionDefinition>, runtime: Option<String>, config: Option<serde_json::Value>, dependencies...
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.doc_comment:
A single task within a workflow.

Supports two schemas:
- **New (runtime)**: `runtime = "http"`, `config = { url = "..." }`
- **Legacy (action)**: `action = { type = "tool", name = "echo" }`

When both are present, `runtime`/`config` take precedence.

# Examples

```rust,ignore
use arawn_pipeline::TaskDefinition;

// runtime schema
let task_toml = r#"
id = "fetch"
runtime = "http"
config = { url = "https://example.com" }
"#;
```

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.name: TaskDefinition
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.doc_comment:
```
on_event = "session_close"
[workflow.triggers]

max_retries = 3
timeout_secs = 300
[workflow.runtime]

timezone = "America/New_York"
cron = "0 9 * * *"
[workflow.schedule]

dependencies = ["extract_entities"]
action = { type = "tool", name = "memory_store" }
id = "store_results"
[[workflow.tasks]]

retry_attempts = 2
action = { type = "tool", name = "llm_generate", params = { prompt = "Extract from: {{input.text}}" } }
id = "extract_entities"
[[workflow.tasks]]

description = "Post-session entity extraction and summarization"
name = "session_indexing"
[workflow]
```toml

and converts them to Cloacina workflows via the core builder API.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.name: effective_runtime
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.doc_comment:
Returns the effective runtime name.

If `runtime` is set, returns it directly. Otherwise derives from `action`:
- `Tool { name, .. }` → name
- `Script { .. }` → "script"
- `Llm { .. }` → "llm"

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.name: effective_config
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.doc_comment:
Returns the effective config value.

If `config` is set, returns it. Otherwise derives from `action.params`.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.name: validate
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.doc_comment:
Validate the workflow definition.

Checks:
- At least one task
- No duplicate task IDs
- All dependency references point to existing tasks
- No cycles in the dependency graph
- Script actions have valid language

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.name: detect_cycles
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.doc_comment: Detect cycles in the task dependency graph using Kahn's algorithm.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.name: ActionExecutorFactory
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.doc_comment:
Type alias for a factory that produces a `TaskFn` from an `ActionDefinition`.

This allows the caller to plug in their own action execution logic
(tool dispatch, script sandbox, LLM calls) without the definition module
knowing the details.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.name: to_dynamic_tasks
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/definition.rs.doc_comment:
Convert this declarative definition into Cloacina `DynamicTask`s.

The `executor_factory` is called for each task to produce the actual
execution function based on the action type. This decouples parsing
from execution — the definition module doesn't know how to run tools,
scripts, or LLM calls.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.name: new
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.doc_comment:
Initialize the pipeline engine with a SQLite database.

# Arguments

* `db_path` - Path to the SQLite database file
* `config` - Engine configuration

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.name: register_workflow
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.doc_comment:
Register a dynamically constructed workflow.

The workflow is built using Cloacina's builder API and registered
with the engine for execution. This does not use macros.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.name: register_dynamic_workflow
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        name: &str,
        description: &str,
        tasks: Vec<DynamicTask>,
    ) -> Result<(), PipelineError>
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.doc_comment:
Build and register a workflow from dynamic tasks.

Convenience method that constructs a workflow from `DynamicTask`s
using Cloacina's builder API.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.name: execute
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.doc_comment:
Execute a registered workflow.

Runs the workflow synchronously (waits for completion) and returns
the result.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.name: trigger
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.doc_comment:
Execute a workflow via push trigger.

This is the same as `execute` but semantically represents an
event-driven invocation (e.g., session close, memory update).

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.name: schedule_cron
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.doc_comment:
Register a cron schedule for a workflow.

# Arguments

* `workflow_name` - Name of the workflow to schedule
* `cron_expr` - Cron expression (e.g., "0 9 * * *" for 9am daily)
* `timezone` - IANA timezone (e.g., "America/New_York")

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.name: list_schedules
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.doc_comment: List all cron schedules.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.name: cancel_schedule
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.doc_comment: Cancel a cron schedule.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.name: list_workflows
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.doc_comment: List registered workflow names.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/engine.rs.name: has_workflow

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/factory.rs.name: build_executor_factory
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/factory.rs.doc_comment:
Build an `ActionExecutorFactory` that dispatches to WASM runtimes via
`ScriptExecutor::execute_runtime`.

Each produced `TaskFn` will:
1. Snapshot the current pipeline context as a JSON `Value`
2. Build a `RuntimeInput { config, context }`
3. Call `execute_runtime` on the `ScriptExecutor`
4. Store the `RuntimeOutput.output` under `context[task_id]`

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/lib.rs.name: catalog
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
This crate provides the `PipelineEngine` — Arawn's execution backbone for
resilient, async, and scheduled workflows. It wraps Cloacina's `DefaultRunner`
with Arawn-specific configuration and a dynamic workflow construction API.

# Architecture

```text
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  PipelineEngine                                         │
│  - Wraps Cloacina DefaultRunner (SQLite backend)        │
│  - Dynamic workflow construction (no macros)            │
│  - Cron scheduling + push triggers                      │
│  - Agent-facing API for workflow CRUD                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

│  PipelineEngine                                         │
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
```text

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.name: ScriptConfig
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.doc_comment:
5. Context flows in via WASI stdin (JSON), output captured from WASI stdout (JSON)
4. `ScriptExecutor::execute()` runs the module in Wasmtime with scoped WASI capabilities
3. WASM binary is cached by SHA-256 of source content
2. `ScriptExecutor::compile()` invokes `rustc --target wasm32-wasip1`
1. Agent generates Rust source code

# Flow

Wasmtime sandbox for executing Rust scripts compiled to `wasm32-wasip1`.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.name: compile
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.doc_comment:
Compile Rust source code to a WASM module targeting `wasm32-wasip1`.

Returns a `CompileResult` with the hash and path. Uses a SHA-256 cache
to skip recompilation for identical source content.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.name: compile_crate
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.doc_comment:
Compile an entire Cargo crate to `wasm32-wasip1` and return the `.wasm` path.

Runs `cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --release` on the given crate directory.
The compiled `.wasm` is left in the crate's `target/` directory.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.name: execute
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.doc_comment:
Execute a previously compiled WASM module with the given context and capabilities.

Context is passed as JSON via stdin. Output is captured from stdout.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.name: compile_and_execute
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        source: &str,
        context_json: &str,
        config: &ScriptConfig,
    ) -> Result<(CompileResult, ScriptOutput), PipelineError>
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.doc_comment: Compile and execute in one call.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.name: clear_cache
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.doc_comment: Clear the in-memory module cache.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.name: execute_runtime
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.doc_comment:
Execute a named runtime from the catalog with the given input.

Looks up the runtime in the catalog, loads and caches the `.wasm` module,
serializes `RuntimeInput` to stdin, and parses stdout as `RuntimeOutput`.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.name: check_wasm_target
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.doc_comment: Check if the `wasm32-wasip1` target is installed.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.name: execute_sync
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.signature:
(
        engine: &Engine,
        module: &Module,
        context_json: &str,
        capabilities: &Capabilities,
        timeout: Duration,
    ) -> Result<ScriptOutput, PipelineError>
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.doc_comment: Synchronous WASM execution with Wasmtime + WASI Preview 1.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.name: sha256_hex
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/sandbox.rs.doc_comment: Compute SHA-256 hex digest of a string.

Type alias for the async function that executes a dynamic task.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/task.rs.name: DynamicTask
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/task.rs.signature: { id: String, dependencies: Vec<TaskNamespace>, retry_policy: RetryPolicy, execute_fn: TaskFn }
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/task.rs.doc_comment:
A task that can be constructed at runtime without macros.

This implements Cloacina's `Task` trait with all parameters provided at
construction time, enabling dynamic workflow definition from TOML files
or agent-generated specifications.

crates/arawn-pipeline/src/task.rs.name: DynamicTask
crates/arawn-pipeline/src/task.rs.doc_comment:
declarative workflow definitions (TOML files) and Cloacina's execution engine.
that can be constructed at runtime without macros. This is the bridge between
Provides `DynamicTask` — a concrete implementation of Cloacina's `Task` trait

Dynamic task implementation for runtime-defined workflows.

* `execute_fn` - Async function to execute

crates/arawn-pipeline/tests/e2e_runtime_.name: can_compile_wasm
crates/arawn-pipeline/tests/e2e_runtime_.doc_comment:
Test 2: Agent self-extension — compile, register, and execute a custom runtime.
Test 1: Multi-step workflow with context propagation between tasks.

End-to-end integration tests for the WASM runtime pipeline.

crates/arawn-pipeline/tests/e2e_runtime_.name: setup
crates/arawn-pipeline/tests/e2e_runtime_.doc_comment:
Set up executor + catalog with compiled test runtimes.

Registers two runtimes:
- `passthrough`: echoes the full RuntimeInput as output
- `uppercase`: reads `config.text`, uppercases it, returns as output

crates/arawn-pipeline/tests/e2e_runtime_.name: test_multistep_workflow_context_propagation
crates/arawn-pipeline/tests/e2e_runtime_.doc_comment:
Test 1: Multi-step workflow with context propagation.

Workflow: passthrough (step1) -> uppercase (step2)
- step1 echoes the input config+context
- step2 reads config.text and uppercases it
- step2 depends on step1

crates/arawn-pipeline/tests/e2e_runtime_.name: test_agent_self_extension
crates/arawn-pipeline/tests/e2e_runtime_.doc_comment:
Test 2: Agent self-extension — compile a custom runtime, register it,
and execute it in a workflow.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.signature: { text: String, truncated: bool, original_len: Option<usize> }
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment: Result of truncating a subagent response.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.name: truncate_result
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment:
Truncate a subagent result, preserving beginning and end of the response.

If the text exceeds `max_len`, keeps the first 60% and last 30% of the budget,
with a truncation notice in between.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment: System prompt for context compaction.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment: Compact a long subagent result using LLM summarization.
