crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.signature: { parent_tools: Arc<ToolRegistry>, backend: SharedBackend, default_max_iterations: Option<u32> }
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment: Spawns agents from plugin agent configurations.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.name: AgentSpawner
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment:
- [`PluginSubagentSpawner`]: Implements [`SubagentSpawner`] trait for use with `DelegateTool`
- [`AgentSpawner`]: Low-level spawner that creates agents from configs
This module provides two main types:

Subagents get constrained tool sets and custom system prompts.
Creates `Agent` instances from plugin-defined agent configurations.

Plugin agent spawning.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.name: new
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment: Create a new agent spawner.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.name: with_default_max_iterations
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new agent spawner with a default max_iterations.

The `default_max_iterations` is applied to all spawned agents unless
they specify their own `constraints.max_iterations`.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.name: spawn
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment:
Spawn an agent from a plugin agent configuration.

The spawned agent gets:
- A constrained tool registry (only tools listed in config)
- The plugin agent's custom system prompt
- Optional max_iterations cap

The max_iterations resolution order is:
1. Agent-specific `constraints.max_iterations` (highest priority)
2. Global `default_max_iterations` from `[agent.default]` config
3. `AgentConfig::default()` (hardcoded 10)

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.name: constrain_tools
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment: Create a constrained tool registry from the parent's tools.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.name: new
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.signature:
(
        parent_tools: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
        backend: SharedBackend,
        agent_configs: HashMap<String, PluginAgentConfig>,
    ) -> Self
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new plugin subagent spawner.

# Arguments
* `parent_tools` - The parent agent's tool registry
* `backend` - The LLM backend to use for subagents
* `agent_configs` - Map of agent name to configuration

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.name: with_sources
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.signature:
(
        parent_tools: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
        backend: SharedBackend,
        agent_configs: HashMap<String, PluginAgentConfig>,
        agent_sources: HashMap<String, String>,
    ) -> Self
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment:
Create a spawner with source plugin tracking.

This variant records which plugin each agent came from,
useful for debugging and the `source` field in [`SubagentInfo`].

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.name: with_hook_dispatcher
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment:
Set the hook dispatcher for subagent lifecycle events.

When set, the spawner will fire `SubagentStarted` and `SubagentCompleted`
events for background subagent executions.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.name: with_compaction
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.signature: (mut self, backend: SharedBackend, config: CompactionConfig) -> Self
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment:
Set the compaction backend and configuration.

When set, long subagent results will be summarized using the provided
LLM backend instead of being truncated.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.name: with_default_max_iterations
crates/arawn-plugin/src/agent_spawner.rs.doc_comment:
Set the default max_iterations for all spawned agents.

This value is used as a fallback when an agent doesn't specify
its own `constraints.max_iterations`. Mirrors `[agent.default].max_iterations`.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/hooks.rs.name: register_from_config
crates/arawn-plugin/src/hooks.rs.doc_comment:
Register hooks from a Claude-format `HooksConfig`.

This converts the Claude `HooksConfig` format (matcher groups with actions)
into `HookDef` entries that the dispatcher can execute.

Only `HookType::Command` hooks are supported; `Prompt` and `Agent` hooks
are logged and skipped.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/hooks.rs.doc_comment:
Implement the HookDispatch trait for HookDispatcher.

This allows HookDispatcher to be used via the trait object pattern,
avoiding cyclic dependencies between arawn-agent and arawn-plugin.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/lib.rs.name: agent_spawner
crates/arawn-plugin/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Plugins bundle skills, hooks, agents, and prompt fragments together with a
JSON manifest. This crate provides the core types, manifest parsing, and
plugin loading infrastructure.

Compatible with Claude Code's plugin format.

# Plugin Structure

```text
my-plugin/
.claude-plugin/
plugin.json            # manifest
skills/
my-skill/
SKILL.md             # skill: prompt template with YAML frontmatter
hooks/
hooks.json             # hook configuration
agents/
my-agent.md            # agent: markdown with YAML frontmatter
```

crates/arawn-plugin/src/lib.rs.name: hooks
crates/arawn-plugin/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
```
my-agent.md            # agent: markdown with YAML frontmatter
agents/
hooks.json             # hook configuration
hooks/
SKILL.md             # skill: prompt template with YAML frontmatter
my-skill/
skills/
plugin.json            # manifest
.claude-plugin/
my-plugin/
```text

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.name: with_defaults

Create a `PluginManager` with default directories.

Scans `~/.config/arawn/plugins/` and `./plugins/`.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.name: plugin_dirs
crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.doc_comment: Get the configured plugin directories.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.name: load_all
crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
Discover and load all plugins from configured directories.

Scans each directory for subdirectories containing `plugin.toml`.
Invalid plugins are logged as warnings and skipped.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.name: scan_directory
crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
Scan a single directory for plugin subdirectories.

A plugin is identified by having a `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` file.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.name: load_plugin
crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
Load a single plugin from its directory.

Discovers skills from `skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, agents from `agents/<name>.md`,
and hooks from `hooks/hooks.json` (or path specified in manifest).

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.name: discover_skills
crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
Discover skills from the skills directories.

Claude format: `skills/<name>/SKILL.md`

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.name: discover_agents

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.name: load_hooks
crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:

Claude format: `hooks/hooks.json` or path specified in manifest `hooks` field.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.name: load_single
crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.doc_comment: Load a single plugin by directory path (for hot-reload).

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.name: extract_frontmatter_field
crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
Extract a field value from YAML frontmatter in a markdown file.

Expects frontmatter delimited by `---` at the start of the file.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.name: parse_agent_markdown
crates/arawn-plugin/src/manager.rs.doc_comment:
Parse an agent configuration from a Claude-format markdown file.

Format:
```markdown
---
description: Agent description
capabilities: ["task1", "task2"]
tools: ["shell", "file_read"]
---

# Agent Name

System prompt / detailed instructions...
```

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manifest.rs.name: validate
crates/arawn-plugin/src/manifest.rs.doc_comment:
Validate required fields and constraints.

This performs comprehensive validation including:
- Name format (kebab-case, starts with letter, etc.)
- Version format (semver if provided)

For path validation (checking declared paths exist), use
[`validate_paths`] after loading from disk.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/manifest.rs.name: validate_paths
crates/arawn-plugin/src/manifest.rs.doc_comment:
Validate that declared paths exist on disk.

This should be called after loading a manifest from a specific plugin directory.
It verifies that all declared component paths (skills, agents, hooks, etc.)
actually exist.

# Arguments

* `plugin_dir` - The root directory of the plugin (containing `.claude-plugin/`)

# Returns

A list of validation errors for any missing paths.

This helps identify mismatches between what a manifest declares
and what's actually available in the plugin directory.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: Skill
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.signature: { name: String, description: String, uses_tools: Vec<String>, args: Vec<SkillArg>, body: String, plugin_name: String }
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment: A parsed skill ready for invocation.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: SkillInvocation
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.signature: { name: String, plugin: Option<String>, raw_args: String }
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment: Result of parsing a `/skill-name args` or `/plugin:skill args` invocation from a user message.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: SkillFrontmatter
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.signature: { name: String, description: String, uses_tools: Vec<String>, args: Vec<SkillArg> }
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment: Frontmatter parsed from a skill markdown file.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: parse_skill
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment:
Parse a skill from its markdown content.

Extracts YAML frontmatter between `---` delimiters and the remaining
markdown body. This follows Claude Code's skill format.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: split_frontmatter
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment: Split markdown content into frontmatter and body.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: detect_invocation
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment:
Detect a skill invocation in a user message.

Matches messages starting with:
- `/skill-name` - simple skill invocation
- `/plugin-name:skill-name` - namespaced skill invocation

Names can contain lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens.
Returns `None` if the message doesn't match.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: substitute_args
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment:
Substitute arguments into a skill body template.

Replaces `{arg_name}` placeholders with provided values.
Positional args (space-separated in raw_args) map to declared arg names in order.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: SkillRegistry
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.signature: { skills: HashMap<String, Skill>, by_simple_name: HashMap<String, Vec<String>> }
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment:
Registry of loaded skills, queryable by name or qualified name.

Skills can be looked up by:
- Simple name: `skill-name` (may be ambiguous if multiple plugins have same skill)
- Qualified name: `plugin-name:skill-name` (unambiguous)

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: new

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: register
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment:
Register a skill.

The skill is stored by its qualified name (`plugin:skill`) and indexed
by its simple name for non-namespaced lookups.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: get
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment:
Look up a skill by name (simple) or qualified name (plugin:skill).

For simple names, returns the skill if unambiguous (only one plugin has it).
For qualified names, returns the exact match.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: get_by_invocation

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: names

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: len
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment: Get the number of registered skills.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: is_empty

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: invoke
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment:
Invoke a skill by invocation with raw arguments.

Returns the rendered skill body with arguments substituted,
or `None` if the skill is not found.

crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.name: invoke_simple
crates/arawn-plugin/src/skill.rs.doc_comment:
Invoke a skill by simple name with raw arguments (convenience method).

Returns the rendered skill body with arguments substituted,
or `None` if the skill is not found.

crates/arawn-sandbox/src/config.rs.name: SandboxConfig
crates/arawn-sandbox/src/config.rs.signature: { write_paths: Vec<PathBuf>, deny_read_paths: Vec<PathBuf>, allowed_domains: Vec<String>, working_dir: Option<PathBuf>, ...
crates/arawn-sandbox/src/config.rs.doc_comment:
Configuration for sandbox execution.

# Security Model

- **Write paths**: Explicitly allowed directories for write access.
Everything else is read-only.
- **Deny read paths**: Sensitive paths that should be blocked even for reading.
- **Network**: Domain-based allowlist for network access.

# Examples

```rust,ignore
use arawn_sandbox::SandboxConfig;
use std::time::Duration;

let config = SandboxConfig::new()
.add_write_path("/tmp/workspace")
.add_allowed_domain("api.github.com")
.with_timeout(Duration::from_secs(60))
.with_working_dir("/tmp/workspace");
```

crates/arawn-server/src/ratelimit.rs.name: extract_client_ip
crates/arawn-server/src/ratelimit.rs.doc_comment:
Extract client IP address from request headers.

Checks in order:
1. X-Forwarded-For header (first IP if multiple)
2. X-Real-IP header
3. ConnectInfo socket address
4. Falls back to 127.0.0.1 if nothing found

crates/arawn-server/src/ratelimit.rs.name: rate_limit_middleware
crates/arawn-server/src/ratelimit.rs.signature:
(
    State(state): State<AppState>,
    request: Request<Body>,
    next: Next,
) -> Response
crates/arawn-server/src/ratelimit.rs.doc_comment:
Rate limiting middleware for API endpoints.

Uses per-IP rate limiting to prevent individual bad actors from affecting
other users. Extracts client IP from X-Forwarded-For, X-Real-IP headers,
or falls back to the connection address.

The rate limiter is created from `config.api_rpm` and stored in AppState.

crates/arawn-server/src/ratelimit.rs.name: request_logging_middleware
crates/arawn-server/src/ratelimit.rs.signature:
(
    State(state): State<AppState>,
    request: Request<Body>,
    next: Next,
) -> Response
crates/arawn-server/src/ratelimit.rs.doc_comment:
Structured request logging middleware.

Logs request details including method, path, status, and duration.
Uses tracing for structured logging that can be captured by log aggregators.

crates/arawn-server/src/routes/mcp.rs.name: AddServerRequest
crates/arawn-server/src/routes/mcp.rs.signature: { name: String, transport: String, command: String, url: Option<String>, args: Vec<String>, env: Vec<(String, String)>, ...
crates/arawn-server/src/routes/mcp.rs.doc_comment:
Request to add a new MCP server.

## Transport Types

### `stdio` (default)
Launches a local process and communicates over stdin/stdout.
- **Required:** `command`
- **Optional:** `args`, `env`
- **Ignored:** `url`, `headers`, `timeout_secs`, `retries`

### `http`
Connects to a remote MCP server over HTTP.
- **Required:** `url`
- **Optional:** `headers`, `timeout_secs`, `retries`
- **Ignored:** `command`, `args`, `env`

crates/arawn-server/src/routes/mcp.rs.name: default_connect
crates/arawn-server/src/routes/mcp.rs.doc_comment:
- `GET /api/v1/mcp/servers/:name/tools` - List tools for a specific server
- `GET /api/v1/mcp/servers` - List all connected servers and their tools
- `DELETE /api/v1/mcp/servers/:name` - Remove an MCP server
- `POST /api/v1/mcp/servers` - Add a new MCP server
Provides REST API for runtime MCP server registration and management:

MCP (Model Context Protocol) server management endpoints.

    conn_state: &mut ConnectionState,
    app_state: &AppState,

crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/handle.doc_comment:
Handle session subscription.

First subscriber to a session becomes the owner (can send Chat messages).
Subsequent subscribers are readers (receive messages but cannot send Chat).
If a reconnect_token is provided, attempts to reclaim ownership after disconnect.

crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/handle.name: handle_unsubscribe

(
    session_id: String,
    conn_state: &mut ConnectionState,
    app_state: &AppState,
) -> MessageResponse
crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/handle.doc_comment:
Handle session unsubscription.

Releases session ownership if this connection was the owner.
Unlike disconnect, explicit unsubscribe does not create a pending reconnect.

    conn_state: &mut ConnectionState,
    app_state: &AppState,

crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/handle.doc_comment:
Handle chat message.

Only the session owner can send chat messages. If no session ID is provided,
a new session is created and this connection becomes the owner.

crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.name: connection
crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.doc_comment:
This module provides WebSocket support for the Arawn server, enabling:
- Real-time chat with streaming responses
- Tool execution status updates
- Session subscription for multi-client scenarios

## Module Structure

- `protocol` - Message types (ClientMessage, ServerMessage)
- `connection` - Connection lifecycle and state management
- `handlers` - Message processing logic

## Security

- Origin header validation prevents CSRF attacks
- Message size limits prevent DoS attacks
- Connection rate limiting prevents connection floods

crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.name: handlers
crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.doc_comment:

- Message size limits prevent DoS attacks
- Origin header validation prevents CSRF attacks

- `connection` - Connection lifecycle and state management
- `protocol` - Message types (ClientMessage, ServerMessage)

## Module Structure

- Session subscription for multi-client scenarios
- Tool execution status updates
- Real-time chat with streaming responses

crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.name: ws_handler
crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.signature:
(
    ws: WebSocketUpgrade,
    headers: HeaderMap,
    ConnectInfo(addr): ConnectInfo<SocketAddr>,
    State(state): State<AppState>,
) -> Response
crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.doc_comment:
GET /ws - WebSocket upgrade handler.

## Security

- Validates Origin header against configured allowed origins
- Rate limits connections per IP address
- Authentication happens via the first message (Auth type) to support browsers

Note: If `ws_allowed_origins` is empty, all origins are allowed (development mode).

crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.name: validate_origin
crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.doc_comment:

crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.name: is_localhost_origin
crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.doc_comment: Check if an origin is a localhost-class origin (no port specified).

crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.name: origin_matches_ignoring_port
crates/arawn-server/src/routes/ws/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Check if an origin matches an allowed origin ignoring port differences.

For example, `http://localhost:3000` matches `http://localhost`.

crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.name: WorkstreamPersistence
crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.doc_comment:
Persistence hook that loads/saves sessions from workstream JSONL storage.

When the cache misses, this hook loads the session from the workstream's
JSONL message store. If no messages are found, it returns an empty session
(matching the previous behavior where a cache miss creates a fresh session).

crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.name: SessionCache
crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.doc_comment:
Session cache that loads from and persists to workstream storage.

Uses LRU eviction to prevent unbounded memory growth. Least recently
used sessions are evicted when the cache reaches capacity. Sessions
can also expire based on TTL if configured.

Backed by [`SessionCacheImpl`] with a [`WorkstreamPersistence`]
hook for workstream JSONL storage.

crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.name: SessionCache
crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.doc_comment:
workstream JSONL storage.

- Persists turn data back to workstream storage
- Optionally expires sessions after a TTL period
- Caches sessions in memory for performance with LRU eviction
- Loads sessions from workstream JSONL on cache miss

the cache, enabling decoupled configuration passing.

crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.doc_comment: Check if the cache is empty.

crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.doc_comment:
Clean up expired sessions.

Returns the number of sessions that were cleaned up.

crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.name: get_or_load
crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.doc_comment:
Get a session from cache or load from workstream.

Returns the session and its associated workstream ID.
This marks the session as recently used in the LRU cache and resets TTL.

crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.name: create_session
crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.doc_comment: Create a new session and add it to the cache.

crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.name: get_or_create
crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        session_id: Option<SessionId>,
        workstream_id: &str,
    ) -> Result<(SessionId, Session, bool)>
crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.doc_comment:
Get or create a session.

If `session_id` is Some and exists in cache, returns it.
If `session_id` is Some but not in cache, attempts to load from workstream.
If `session_id` is None, creates a new session.

crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.name: contains
crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.doc_comment: Check if a session exists in cache (and is not expired).

crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.name: get
crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.doc_comment:
Get a session from cache only (no workstream loading).
Does NOT update LRU order (peek operation).

crates/arawn-server/src/session_cache.rs.name: get_workstream_id

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Mutable state that changes during operation.

This state is modified by handlers during normal server operation.
Each field uses appropriate synchronization primitives.

# Lock Ordering

When acquiring multiple locks, always follow:
`pending_reconnects` < `session_owners` < `session_cache` < `tasks`

See the module-level documentation for the full ordering including
`SharedServices` locks.

- `SharedServices`: Immutable services created at startup
State is separated into two layers:

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: AppState
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Application state shared across all handlers.

Composed of immutable `SharedServices` and mutable `RuntimeState`.
This separation provides:
- Clearer ownership semantics
- Better lock granularity (runtime state has finer-grained locks)
- Easier testing (services can be mocked independently)

- Prefer `read()` over `write()` when mutation is not needed.
- Release locks before spawning tasks that acquire locks.
**Guidelines:**

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Configure session cache using a config provider.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: build_domain_services
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Build domain services from the configured components.

This should be called after all services are configured to create
the unified DomainServices facade.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: agent
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the agent.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: config

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the rate limiter.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: workstreams
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the workstream manager.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: indexer
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the session indexer.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: hook_dispatcher
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the hook dispatcher.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: mcp_manager
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the MCP manager.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: directory_manager
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the directory manager.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: sandbox_manager

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: file_watcher

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: memory_store
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the memory store.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: domain
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the domain services facade.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: compressor
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the compressor.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: session_cache
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the session cache.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: tasks
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the task store.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: session_owners
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the session owners.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: pending_reconnects
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the pending reconnects.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: ws_connection_tracker
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get the WebSocket connection tracker.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: check_ws_connection_rate
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Check WebSocket connection rate for an IP address.

Returns `Ok(())` if the connection is allowed, `Err(Response)` if rate limited.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: allowed_paths
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Get allowed paths for a session based on its workstream.

Returns `None` if no directory manager is configured.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: path_validator
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Get a PathValidator for a session.

Returns `None` if no directory manager is configured.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: get_or_create_session
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Get or create a session by ID.

If session_id is None, creates a new session.
Defaults to "scratch" workstream.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: get_or_create_session_in_workstream
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Get or create a session in a specific workstream.

Sessions are loaded from workstream storage on cache miss and persisted back.
For scratch workstreams, also creates the session's isolated work directory.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: close_session
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Close a session: remove it from the cache and trigger background indexing/compression.

Returns `true` if the session existed and was removed.
Indexing and compression run asynchronously and do not block the caller.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: get_session
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Get session from cache (loading from workstream if needed).

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: update_session
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Update session in cache.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: invalidate_session
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Invalidate a cached session (e.g., after workstream reassignment).

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: try_claim_session_ownership
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Try to claim ownership of a session for a connection.

Returns `true` if the connection is now the owner (either it was already,
or it successfully claimed ownership of an unowned session).
Returns `false` if another connection owns this session or if there's
a pending reconnect for the session (ownership reserved for reconnection).

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: is_session_owner
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Check if a connection owns a session.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: release_session_ownership
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Release ownership of a session.

Only the current owner can release ownership.
Returns `true` if ownership was released.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: release_all_session_ownerships
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Release all session ownerships held by a connection, creating pending reconnects.

Called when a WebSocket connection disconnects. Instead of immediately releasing
ownership, creates pending reconnect entries that allow the client to reclaim
ownership within the grace period using the provided tokens.

`reconnect_tokens` maps session IDs to the tokens that were given to the client.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: try_reclaim_with_token
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        session_id: SessionId,
        token: &str,
        connection_id: ConnectionId,
    ) -> Option<String>
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Try to reclaim session ownership using a reconnect token.

Returns `Some(new_token)` if ownership was successfully reclaimed.
Returns `None` if the token is invalid or expired.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: cleanup_expired_pending_reconnects
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment:
Clean up expired pending reconnects.

Called lazily during subscribe operations. Returns the number cleaned up.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: has_pending_reconnect
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Check if a session has a pending reconnect (ownership held for reconnection).

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: session_to_messages
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Convert a session's turns into owned `(role, content)` pairs.

crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.name: messages_as_refs
crates/arawn-server/src/state.rs.doc_comment: Convert owned message pairs to borrowed slices for the indexer API.

crates/arawn-server/tests/validation_int.name: common
crates/arawn-server/tests/validation_int.doc_comment:
These tests verify that validation works correctly at interface boundaries,
testing malformed inputs across plugin, tool, LLM, and memory interfaces
to ensure graceful error handling.

# Test Categories

- Plugin manifest validation (malformed manifests rejected at load time)
- Tool parameter validation (invalid parameters rejected before execution)
- LLM response validation (malformed responses handled gracefully)
- Memory operation validation (invalid data returns proper errors)
- Output sanitization (oversized/binary content handled correctly)

crates/arawn-session/src/cache.rs.name: SessionCache
crates/arawn-session/src/cache.rs.doc_comment:
Session cache with LRU eviction and optional TTL.

This cache provides:
- LRU eviction when max capacity is reached
- Optional TTL-based expiration
- Persistence hooks for loading/saving sessions
- Thread-safe access via RwLock

The value type stored in the cache is determined by the persistence
hook's associated `Value` type. With [`NoPersistence`], values are
[`SessionData`]; custom hooks can store any `Clone + Send + Sync` type.

# Examples

```rust,ignore
use arawn_session::{SessionCache, CacheConfig};

let config = CacheConfig::new().with_max_sessions(100);
let cache = SessionCache::new(config);

cache.insert("session-1", "workstream-1", session_data).await?;
let data = cache.get_or_load("session-1", "workstream-1").await?;
```

crates/arawn-session/src/persistence.rs.signature: { fn load(), fn save(), fn delete(), fn on_evict() }
crates/arawn-session/src/persistence.rs.doc_comment:
Trait for persistence backends.

Implement this trait to connect the session cache to your storage backend.
The cache calls these methods on cache misses and when saving sessions.

The associated `Value` type determines what the cache stores in memory.
For example, a server might use `Value = Session` (a rich domain object),
while a simpler backend might use `Value = SessionData` (opaque bytes).

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: scroll_chat_up
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment:
Scroll chat up by the given number of lines.

Disables auto-scroll so the user can read history without
being snapped back to the bottom during streaming. Auto-scroll
is only re-enabled when the user sends a new message.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: scroll_chat_down
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment:
Scroll chat down by the given number of lines.

Disables auto-scroll to preserve manual scroll position.
Use Ctrl+End or send a message to re-enable auto-scroll.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: Update the command popup based on current input.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: send_command
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: Send the current input as a command.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: build_command_args
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: Build command arguments JSON from parsed command.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: get_help_text
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: Get help text for available commands.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: send_message
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: Send the current input as a chat message.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: open_tool_in_editor
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment:
Open the selected tool's output in an external pager.

This suspends the TUI, runs the pager (e.g., `less`), and restores
the TUI when the user exits the pager.

crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.name: run_pager
crates/arawn-tui/src/app.rs.doc_comment: Run a pager with the given content, suspending and restoring the TUI.

crates/arawn-tui/src/bounded.rs.name: BoundedVec
crates/arawn-tui/src/bounded.rs.doc_comment:
A vector with a maximum capacity that evicts oldest elements when full.

When the vector reaches capacity, adding a new element removes the oldest
10% of elements to make room efficiently (avoiding per-element eviction overhead).

crates/arawn-tui/src/bounded.rs.name: new
crates/arawn-tui/src/bounded.rs.doc_comment:
Create a new bounded vector with the specified maximum capacity.

# Panics
Panics if `max_capacity` is 0.

crates/arawn-tui/src/bounded.rs.name: push
crates/arawn-tui/src/bounded.rs.doc_comment:
Push an element, evicting oldest elements if at capacity.

When at capacity, removes the oldest 10% of elements (minimum 1)
to make room efficiently.

crates/arawn-tui/src/input.rs.name: exit_history_mode
crates/arawn-tui/src/input.rs.doc_comment:
Exit history browsing mode without restoring draft.

This clears both the history index and the saved draft. Once the user
starts editing (typing, deleting, etc.), they are committed to the
current content and can no longer navigate back to their original draft.
This is intentional: the draft is only for temporary storage while
browsing history, not for persisting across editing operations.

crates/arawn-tui/src/sidebar.rs.name: Sidebar
crates/arawn-tui/src/sidebar.rs.signature: { open: bool, section: SidebarSection, workstreams: Vec<WorkstreamEntry>, workstream_index: usize, sessions: Vec<Session...
crates/arawn-tui/src/sidebar.rs.doc_comment:
Sidebar state managing workstreams and sessions lists.

The sidebar has two states:
- **Closed**: Shows a minimal hint line, not interactive
- **Open**: Full expanded view, has focus and is interactive

Sessions list always has "+ New Session" at index 0.
Actual sessions are at indices 1+.

# Examples

```rust,ignore
use arawn_tui::sidebar::Sidebar;

let mut sidebar = Sidebar::new();
sidebar.toggle(); // open
sidebar.toggle_section(); // switch from Workstreams to Sessions
sidebar.select_next(); // move selection down
```

crates/arawn-types/src/fs_gate.rs.signature: { fn validate_read(), fn validate_write(), fn working_dir(), fn sandbox_execute() }
crates/arawn-types/src/fs_gate.rs.doc_comment:
Filesystem access gate that enforces workstream boundaries.

The sandbox boundary is the **workstream**, not the session:
- Named workstreams: tools access `production/` + `work/` (shared across sessions)
- Scratch: tools are isolated to `scratch/sessions/<id>/work/`

crates/arawn-types/src/fs_gate.rs.doc_comment:
Resolver that creates an FsGate for a given session and workstream.

Takes `(session_id, workstream_id)` and returns an FsGate scoped to
that workstream's boundaries. Returns `None` if no gate can be created
(e.g., no directory manager configured).

crates/arawn-types/src/secret_resolver.r.doc_comment:
Resolver that looks up secrets by name.

Tool parameters may contain opaque handles like `${{secrets.github_token}}`.
The `ToolRegistry` resolves these handles at execution time by calling
`resolve()`, passing the real value to the tool while logging only the handle.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/cleanup.rs.name: cleanup_scratch_sessions
crates/arawn-workstream/src/cleanup.rs.doc_comment:
Clean up inactive scratch sessions.

Sessions are considered inactive if their last activity was more than
`config.scratch_cleanup_days` days ago.

# Arguments

* `dir_manager` - Directory manager for file operations
* `workstream_manager` - Workstream manager for session queries
* `config` - Cleanup configuration

# Returns

Result of the cleanup operation including number of sessions cleaned.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/cleanup.rs.name: check_disk_pressure
crates/arawn-workstream/src/cleanup.rs.doc_comment:
Check disk pressure across workstreams.

# Arguments

* `dir_manager` - Directory manager for usage queries
* `workstream_manager` - Workstream manager for listing workstreams
* `config` - Cleanup configuration with thresholds

# Returns

Result including any pressure events detected.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..name: CompressorConfig
crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..signature: { model: String, max_summary_tokens: u32, token_threshold_chars: usize }

crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..name: Compressor
crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..doc_comment:
Map-reduce context compressor.

- **Map**: Summarize individual sessions.
- **Reduce**: Merge session summaries into a workstream summary.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..name: compress_session
crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..doc_comment:
Compress a single session's messages into a summary.

Reads messages from JSONL for the session's time range,
sends them to the LLM, and stores the summary in SQLite.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..name: compress_workstream
crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..doc_comment:
Reduce all session summaries for a workstream into a single workstream summary.

Reads session summaries from SQLite, sends them to the LLM,
and stores the result as the workstream summary.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..name: needs_compression
crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..doc_comment:
Check if a workstream's current session exceeds the token threshold.

Note: Uses character count as a rough approximation for tokens.
Actual token count varies by model (~4 chars/token for English text),
but character count provides a fast, conservative estimate without
requiring tokenization. The threshold is set accordingly (~8k tokens ≈ 32k chars).

crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..name: summarize
crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..doc_comment: Send messages to LLM with a system prompt for summarization.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..name: filter_session_messages
crates/arawn-workstream/src/compression..doc_comment: Filter messages that belong to a specific session's time range.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.name: AssembledContext
crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.doc_comment: Assembled context ready for injection into an LLM request.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.name: ContextMessage
crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.doc_comment: A message prepared for LLM context, with role mapped to user/assistant.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.name: ContextAssembler
crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.doc_comment: Assembles workstream history into LLM-ready context.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.name: assemble
crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.doc_comment:
Assemble context for a workstream, fitting within `max_chars` (approximate token budget).

Uses char_count / 4 as a rough token estimate. Returns the workstream summary
(if present) plus the most recent messages that fit.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.name: map_role
crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.doc_comment: Map a WorkstreamMessage role to a ContextRole.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.name: fit_messages
crates/arawn-workstream/src/context.rs.doc_comment:
Select the most recent messages that fit within `budget` characters.
Walks backwards from the end, accumulating until budget is exceeded.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/cl.name: clone_repo
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/cl.doc_comment:
Clones a git repository into the workstream's `production/` directory.

This uses the system `git` command, relying on the user's SSH keys
and credential helpers for authentication.

# Arguments

* `workstream` - The workstream name.
* `url` - The git repository URL (HTTPS or SSH).
* `name` - Optional custom directory name. If not provided, derived from URL.

# Returns

A `CloneResult` containing the clone path and HEAD commit hash.

# Errors

- `DirectoryError::InvalidName` if the workstream name is invalid.
- `DirectoryError::WorkstreamNotFound` if the workstream doesn't exist.
- `DirectoryError::AlreadyExists` if the destination directory already exists.
- `DirectoryError::GitNotFound` if git is not installed.
- `DirectoryError::CloneFailed` if the clone operation fails.

# Example

```no_run
use arawn_workstream::directory::DirectoryManager;

let manager = DirectoryManager::default();
let result = manager.clone_repo(
"my-project",
"https://github.com/user/repo.git",
Some("my-repo"),
).unwrap();
println!("Cloned to: {}, commit: {}", result.path.display(), result.commit);
```

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/cl.name: repo_name_from_url
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/cl.doc_comment:
Derive repository name from URL.

Examples:
- `https://github.com/user/repo.git` -> `repo`
- `git@github.com:user/repo.git` -> `repo`
- `https://github.com/user/repo` -> `repo`

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/cl.doc_comment: Check if git is available on the system.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/cl.name: get_head_commit
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/cl.doc_comment: Get the HEAD commit hash for a repository.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.name: is_valid_name
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.doc_comment:
Checks if a workstream name is valid.

Valid names:
- Are not empty
- Contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores
- Do not start with a hyphen or period
- Do not contain path separators

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.name: is_valid_session_id
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.doc_comment:
Checks if a session ID is valid.

Valid session IDs follow the same rules as workstream names.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.name: validate_workstream_id
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.doc_comment:
Validate a workstream ID, returning an error if invalid.

This is the `Result`-returning counterpart to `is_valid_name()`,
intended for use at API boundaries where an error message is needed.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.name: validate_session_id
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.doc_comment: Validate a session ID, returning an error if invalid.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.name: workstream_exists
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.doc_comment: Checks if a workstream exists (has a directory).

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.name: allowed_paths
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.doc_comment:
Returns the allowed paths for a session based on its workstream.

# Access Rules

- **Scratch workstream**: Sessions get isolated paths under
`scratch/sessions/<session-id>/work/`
- **Named workstreams**: Sessions share `<workstream>/production/` and
`<workstream>/work/` directories

# Returns

A vector of `PathBuf` representing the allowed directories for the session.
These paths may not exist yet; use `create_workstream` or `create_scratch_session`
to ensure they exist.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.name: create_workstream
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.doc_comment:
Creates a workstream directory structure.

Creates:
- `<workstream>/production/`
- `<workstream>/work/`

This operation is idempotent; calling it multiple times has no effect
if the directories already exist.

# Arguments

* `name` - The workstream name. Must be valid per `is_valid_name`.

# Returns

The path to the workstream root directory.

# Errors

Returns `DirectoryError::InvalidName` if the name is invalid.
Returns `DirectoryError::Io` if directory creation fails.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.name: create_scratch_session
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.doc_comment:
Creates a scratch session's isolated work directory.

Creates `scratch/sessions/<session-id>/work/`

This operation is idempotent.

# Arguments

* `session_id` - The session ID. Must be valid per `is_valid_session_id`.

# Returns

The path to the session's work directory.

# Errors

Returns `DirectoryError::InvalidSessionId` if the session ID is invalid.
Returns `DirectoryError::Io` if directory creation fails.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.name: remove_scratch_session
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.doc_comment:
Removes a scratch session's directory tree.

# Safety

This permanently deletes all files in the session's work directory.
Use with caution.

# Errors

Returns `DirectoryError::Io` if deletion fails.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.name: list_workstreams
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/ma.doc_comment: Lists all workstream names that have directories (excluding scratch).

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/op.name: promote
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/op.doc_comment:
Promotes a file from `work/` to `production/`.

This moves a file from the workstream's work directory to its production
directory. If a file already exists at the destination, a conflict suffix
is appended (e.g., `file(1).txt`, `file(2).txt`).

# Arguments

* `workstream` - The workstream name.
* `source` - Path relative to the work directory.
* `destination` - Path relative to the production directory.

# Returns

A `PromoteResult` containing the final path, file size, and whether
the file was renamed due to a conflict.

# Errors

- `DirectoryError::InvalidName` if the workstream name is invalid.
- `DirectoryError::WorkstreamNotFound` if the workstream doesn't exist.
- `DirectoryError::SourceNotFound` if the source file doesn't exist.
- `DirectoryError::NotAFile` if the source path is not a file.
- `DirectoryError::Io` if the move operation fails.

# Example

```no_run
use std::path::Path;
use arawn_workstream::directory::DirectoryManager;

let manager = DirectoryManager::default();
let result = manager.promote(
"my-blog",
Path::new("draft.md"),
Path::new("posts/final.md"),
).unwrap();
println!("Promoted to: {}", result.path.display());
```

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/op.name: resolve_conflict
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/op.doc_comment:
Resolves a filename conflict by appending a suffix.

Given `file.txt`, tries `file(1).txt`, `file(2).txt`, etc.
until finding a path that doesn't exist.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/op.name: export
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/op.doc_comment:
Exports a file from `production/` to an external path.

This copies a file from the workstream's production directory to an
arbitrary external location. The source file is preserved (not moved).

# Arguments

* `workstream` - The workstream name.
* `source` - Path relative to the production directory.
* `destination` - Absolute external path. If a directory, the source
filename is appended.

# Returns

An `ExportResult` containing the final destination path and file size.

# Errors

- `DirectoryError::InvalidName` if the workstream name is invalid.
- `DirectoryError::WorkstreamNotFound` if the workstream doesn't exist.
- `DirectoryError::SourceNotFound` if the source file doesn't exist.
- `DirectoryError::NotAFile` if the source path is not a file.
- `DirectoryError::Io` if the copy operation fails.

# Security Note

The destination path is outside the sandbox. Users are responsible for
choosing safe destinations.

# Example

```no_run
use std::path::Path;
use arawn_workstream::directory::DirectoryManager;

let manager = DirectoryManager::default();
let result = manager.export(
"my-blog",
Path::new("report.pdf"),
Path::new("/mnt/dropbox/reports/"),
).unwrap();
println!("Exported to: {}", result.path.display());
```

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/se.name: DirectoryManager
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/se.doc_comment: Session attachment: migrating scratch sessions to named workstreams.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/se.name: attach_session
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/se.doc_comment:
Attaches a scratch session to a named workstream by migrating its files.

This moves all files from the scratch session's work directory to a
session-named subfolder in the target workstream's work directory.
The empty scratch session directory is cleaned up afterward.

# Arguments

* `session_id` - The session ID being migrated.
* `target_workstream` - The workstream to attach the session to.

# Returns

An `AttachResult` containing the count of migrated files and new paths.

# Errors

- `DirectoryError::InvalidSessionId` if the session ID is invalid.
- `DirectoryError::InvalidName` if the workstream name is invalid.
- `DirectoryError::WorkstreamNotFound` if the target workstream doesn't exist.
- `DirectoryError::SessionWorkNotFound` if the scratch session has no work directory.
- `DirectoryError::Io` if file operations fail.

# Example

```no_run
use arawn_workstream::directory::DirectoryManager;

let manager = DirectoryManager::default();
let result = manager.attach_session("session-123", "my-blog").unwrap();
println!("Migrated {} files to {:?}", result.files_migrated, result.new_work_path);
```

crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/us.name: cleanup_work
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/us.signature:
(
        &self,
        workstream: &str,
        older_than_days: Option<u32>,
        confirmed: bool,
    ) -> DirectoryResult<ManualCleanupResult>
crates/arawn-workstream/src/directory/us.doc_comment:
Clean up files in the work directory.

This method only cleans the `work/` directory, never `production/`.
For scratch workstreams, it cleans session work directories.

# Arguments

* `workstream` - The workstream name.
* `older_than_days` - Optional: only delete files older than this many days.
* `confirmed` - Whether the user has confirmed large deletions.

# Returns

A `ManualCleanupResult` containing:
- `deleted_files`: Number of files actually deleted.
- `freed_bytes`: Total bytes freed.
- `pending_files`: Files that would be deleted if confirmed.
- `requires_confirmation`: True if >100 files would be deleted and not confirmed.

If `requires_confirmation` is true and `confirmed` is false, no files are deleted.
Call again with `confirmed=true` to proceed with the deletion.

# Errors

- `DirectoryError::InvalidName` if the workstream name is invalid.
- `DirectoryError::WorkstreamNotFound` if the workstream doesn't exist.
- `DirectoryError::Io` if file operations fail.

# Safety

This method NEVER deletes from `production/` - only from `work/` directories.

# Example

```no_run
use arawn_workstream::directory::DirectoryManager;

let manager = DirectoryManager::default();

// First call - check if confirmation is needed
let result = manager.cleanup_work("my-project", Some(7), false).unwrap();
if result.requires_confirmation {
println!("Would delete {} files ({:.2} MB)", result.pending_files, result.freed_mb());
// Get user confirmation, then:
let result = manager.cleanup_work("my-project", Some(7), true).unwrap();
}
```

crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.name: validate
crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.doc_comment:
Validate a path for read operations.

The path must exist and resolve (via canonicalization) to a location
within one of the allowed paths and not within any denied paths.

# Returns

The canonicalized path if validation succeeds.

# Errors

- `PathError::NotAllowed` if the path is outside allowed directories
- `PathError::DeniedPath` if the path is in a denied directory
- `PathError::SymlinkEscape` if a symlink points outside allowed directories
- `PathError::Io` if the path cannot be canonicalized

crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.name: validate_write
crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.doc_comment:
Validate a path for write operations.

For files that don't exist yet, validates the parent directory exists
and is within allowed boundaries, then constructs the full path.

If the target file already exists and is a symlink, the symlink target
is resolved and checked against boundaries to prevent symlink-based
sandbox escape on overwrites.

# Algorithm

1. If target exists, canonicalize it and check against boundaries
2. Get the parent directory of the path
3. Canonicalize the parent (must exist)
4. Check parent against denied paths
5. Check parent against allowed paths
6. Return parent + filename

# Returns

The validated path (parent canonicalized + original filename).

# Errors

- `PathError::Invalid` if the path has no parent or filename
- `PathError::ParentNotFound` if the parent directory doesn't exist
- `PathError::NotAllowed` if the parent is outside allowed directories
- `PathError::DeniedPath` if the parent is in a denied directory
- `PathError::SymlinkEscape` if the target is a symlink pointing outside boundaries

crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.name: check_denied
crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.doc_comment: Check if a path is within any denied directory.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.name: check_allowed
crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.doc_comment: Check if a path is within any allowed directory.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.name: is_under_allowed_canonical
crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.doc_comment:
Check if a path is under an allowed directory (using canonicalized allowed paths).

This handles cases like macOS where /var symlinks to /private/var.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.name: validate_for_shell
crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.doc_comment:
Validate that a path is safe for shell execution.

This is a stricter check that ensures the path:
1. Is within allowed boundaries
2. Does not contain shell metacharacters
3. Is not a symlink (to prevent TOCTOU attacks)

# Returns

The canonicalized path if validation succeeds.

PathValidator
crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.doc_comment:
Create a PathValidator from a DirectoryManager for a specific session.

allowed paths for a workstream/session from the DirectoryManager.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/path_validat.doc_comment:
Create a validator for a specific workstream and session.

Uses `DirectoryManager::allowed_paths()` to get the allowed directories.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/scratch.rs.doc_comment: Manages the scratch workstream and promotion to named workstreams.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/scratch.rs.doc_comment: Ensure the scratch workstream exists, creating it if missing.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/scratch.rs.name: promote
crates/arawn-workstream/src/scratch.rs.doc_comment:
Promote the scratch workstream to a named workstream.

Creates a new workstream, moves JSONL history and SQLite records
from scratch to the new workstream, then resets scratch to empty.

Returns the new workstream.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/session.rs.signature: { store: &'a WorkstreamStore, message_store: &'a MessageStore, timeout: Duration }
crates/arawn-workstream/src/session.rs.doc_comment:

crates/arawn-workstream/src/session.rs.name: get_or_start_session
crates/arawn-workstream/src/session.rs.doc_comment:
Get or start a session for the workstream.

If an active session exists and is not timed out, returns it.
If the active session is timed out, ends it and starts a new one.
If no active session exists, starts a new one.

crates/arawn-workstream/src/session.rs.name: timeout_check
crates/arawn-workstream/src/session.rs.doc_comment:
Scan for and end all timed-out sessions across all workstreams.
Returns the number of sessions that were timed out.

interchangeably.

MessageStorage (trait)          - Message append/read operations

└── MockWorkstreamStorage   - In-memory mock for testing
└── SqliteWorkstreamStore   - Default SQLite implementation
WorkstreamStorage (trait)       - Workstream CRUD operations
```text

crates/arawn-workstream/src/watcher.rs.name: start
crates/arawn-workstream/src/watcher.rs.doc_comment:
Start watching and return a receiver for events.

The watcher runs in a background thread and emits `FsChangeEvent`s
when files change in watched workstream directories.

# Arguments

* `workstreams` - List of workstream IDs to watch

# Returns

A tuple of (event receiver, watcher handle). The handle must be kept
alive to continue watching. Events are sent to the receiver channel.

crates/arawn/src/main.rs.name: resolve_server_url
crates/arawn/src/main.rs.doc_comment:
Resolve the server URL from various sources.

Priority order:
1. CLI `--server` flag (already checked by clap via `cli.server`)
2. `--context` flag → lookup in client config
3. Current context from client config
4. ARAWN_SERVER_URL environment variable (already checked by clap)
5. Default: http://localhost:8080

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/lib.rs.name: model
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
zero-shot [Named Entity Recognition](https://paperswithcode.com/task/cg) (NER) and many other tasks such
as [Relation Extraction](https://paperswithcode.com/task/relation-extraction).

This implementation supports both span- and token-oriented models (inference only).

It aims to provide an efficient, production-grade, user-friendly API in a modern and safe programming language,
as well as a clean and maintainable implementation of the mechanics surrounding the model itself.

For NER, they will normally be applied in that order:
- Text input (raw entities and texts)
- Tokenized input (unchanged entities with tokenized texts)
- Prompts with entities tokens + text word-level tokens
- Encoded prompts applying sub-word tokenization to text tokens
- Ready for inference tensors

Other steps are for use in pipelines for other applications:
- Input for relation extraction
- ...

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/input.name: make_entity_labels
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/input.doc_comment:
Build entity-text -> entity-labels map (which will be used when decoding, to filter relations basing on allowed objects).

Multiple labels for the same entity text is supported, but in this case there is no guarantee that a
relation actually mentions an entity as having a given label since we just have this information
(limitation of GLiNER multi). So, as soon as one expected class is found for an entity, it will have
to be accepted without knowing its actual class within the relation (which is probably ok).

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/input.name: make_spans_tensors
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/input.doc_comment:
Expected tensor for num_words=4 and max_width=12:
```text
start, end, mask
0, 0, true
0, 1, true
0, 2, true
0, 3, true
0, 0, false
[...until we have all the 12 spans for this token]
1, 1, true
1, 2, true
1, 3, true
0, 0, false
[...]
2, 2, true
2, 3, true
0, 0, false
[...]
3, 3, true
0, 0, false
[...]
```

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.name: new
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.signature: (flat_ner: bool, dup_label: bool, multi_label: bool) -> Self
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.doc_comment:
Creates a new greedy-search performer

Arguments:
* `flat_ner`: if `true`, a span is not allowed not embed another one
* `multi_label`: if `true`, the same span can belong to multiple classes

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.name: search
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.doc_comment:
Perform greedy search

Note: spans are supposed to be sorted by start, and then end, offsets.

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.name: accept
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.doc_comment:
Returns `true` iif the span is valid wrt. the provided flags.

Namely:
* All overlapping spans are forbiden if `flat_ner` is `true`.
* Otherwise, `dup_label=true` allows for overlapping spans with the *same* label.
* And `multi_label=true` allows for overlapping spans with *different* labels.

The checks are only relative to the previous span, as we expect them to be sorted by offset.

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.name: TensorsToDecoded
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.doc_comment:
Decoding method for token mode.

From the related (GLiNER multi-task) [paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2406.12925v1):

> The function `ϕi(k,c)` is the inside score for the token at position `k` within the span,
> indicating the likelihood of the token being part of the entity class `c`. By averaging
> these scores over the span, we obtain a measure of how well the span fits the token class `c`.

Rq: greedy search must be applied the same way as in span mode (shall be called as a subsequent
step in the pipeline).

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.doc_comment: First step of span decoding (in token mode)

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.name: generate_spans
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.doc_comment:
Generates all possible `(i,j,c)` spans where:
* `i <= j`
* `score(i) >= threshold`
* `score(j) >= threshold`.
* `c` == `class(i) == class(j)`

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.name: compute_span_score
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.doc_comment:
Computes the score of a span, defined as the mean of the inside scores (see above).
Spans with one or more inside scores below the threshold will return a zero score,
since they should be discarded.

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.name: check_shape
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.doc_comment:
Checks coherence of the output shape.
Expected shape is (3, batch_size, num_words, num_classes).
The first dimension is related to `start`, `end` and `inside` positions in that order.

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.name: FlatTokenDecoder
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/model/outpu.doc_comment:
*Experimental* token decoding with a one-dimensional approach, working directly on a flat representation of
the model output, with one padding by dimension to access appropriate value. Not very readable, but might
be interresting from a performance standpoint. To be benchmarked, and checked for accurracy according to
the original implementation. In the meantime, prefer the `token.rs` which performs the same operation in
a much more readable way, basing on the four-dimensional output tensor.

crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/util/error..name: IndexError
crates/gline-rs-vendored/src/util/error..doc_comment:
Defines an error caused by the use of an incorrect index in one of the
structures exchanged during a pipeline. This is an internal error that
only occurs if there is a bug in the algorithms ensuring the consistency
of this data, and it cannot be recovered from except by abandoning the
ongoing process and report an error. However, it is preferred over panicking
to ensure safe usage of the library (please document an issue in this case,
providing the message available in this struct).

runtimes/http/src/main.rs.name: RuntimeInput
runtimes/http/src/main.rs.doc_comment:
In standalone WASI preview 2 environments, this would use wasi-http directly.

The host-side executor intercepts and performs the actual HTTP call.
delegates actual execution to the host via stdout protocol.
a minimal approach: it constructs the request representation and

provide command execution capability via WASI extensions or a compatible

Note: Under WASI preview 1, process spawning is not natively supported.

At least one of `expression`, `template`, or `mappings` must be provided.

expressions to extract, building a new output object
- `mappings` (object, optional): Key-value pairs where values are dot-path
that are resolved against the context
- `template` (string, optional): Template string with `{{expression}}` placeholders
(e.g., "task1.body", "step2.result.items")
- `expression` (string, optional): Dot-path expression to extract from context
Config fields:

Transform runtime — dot-path traversal and template interpolation on context data.
